observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an actual count of
inventories, prepare the RPCI, and submit the same to the Office of the
Auditor, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS;
b. Ensure that CGSO maintains and consolidates property records of all
inventory accounts, and the CGSO and the City Accountant reconcile
both property and accounting records;
c. In coordination with CGSO, institutionalize the use of RIS in accordance
with the inventory process in the control of inventory detailed in Section
122 of the Manual on NGAS;
d. Instruct the CGSO to consolidate weekly the RIS using the SSMI and
submit the same to the Accountant as the basis for proper recording of
expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts; and
e. Examine all dormant accounts and, where applicable, request for relief
of accountability for inventories that are lost, missing, and/or cannot be
located.

We recommended and Management agreed to enjoin the concerned
department heads to take the following actions to ensure compliance with
COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015, and improve the
accuracy and reliability of the Road Networks account:
a. The Inventory Committee conducts an annual physical count of all LRN,
and the results thereof are reported in the RPCLRN, which is to be
submitted to the Auditor and Accounting unit not later than January 31
of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting
records, and the necessary adjusting entries must be prepared before the
statement date;
b. The City Engineer provides the City Accountant and the CGSO with a
complete description and segregation of all road components for road
projects, ensuring proper cost allocation and asset valuation;
c. The CGSO keeps complete and updated LRNPC for all roads and their
components, ensuring compliance with the required documentation
standards; and
d. The City Accountant maintains the LRNLC, provides depreciation for all
depriciable components of the road network, and discloses the total
road network system in the Notes to the FS, following the prescribed
format in COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Budget Officer, in
coordination with the City Accountant, enforce and institutionalize the proper use
of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 when consolidating
the City’s annual budget by developing comprehensive SOPs that outline the
proper procedures for budget preparation, expense classification and monitoring
for each office, unit or department. This will ensure proper and appropriate
recording of all expenditures in the books of accounts in compliance with the
IPSASs.

We recommended and Management agreed that the concerned personnel at
the CAO, in coordination with the Sectoral Desk Office and City Social Welfare
and Development Office (CSWDO):
a. Conduct a thorough review of outstanding balances and consider factors
such as the age of receivables and any specific circumstances that affect
collectability. If necessary, establish impairment allowances to reflect the
real recoverable value of this account;
b. Investigate and conduct a full review of the balances under Due from
NGOs/POs account, including a thorough examination of Memorandum of
Agreements to determine the validity and status of each grant, and take
appropriate legal action against defaulting NGOs/POs in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007;
c. Upon assessing the condition and status of each grant, establish a
comprehensive monitoring schedule to track progress and facilitate
actions for resolutions of the same;
d. Reclassify the unidentified entries and non-interest-bearing notes,
amounting ₱8,571,088.34 and ₱2,075,190.60, respectively, currently
recorded under Due from NGOs/POs to Other Receivables account; and
e. Consider implementing the one-time cleansing of all dormant accounts
under COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Engineer and City
Accountant designate personnel to oversee the verification of project
completion under the 20% EDF, through comprehensive review and
retrieval of relevant documents to facilitate the timely liquidation process and
ensure accurate recording of completed projects.

We further recommended and Management agreed that the City
Accountant, in coordination with the CEO, CGS Officer, and City Budget
Officer obtain the required documentation of the identified completed
projects amounting to ₱45,718,667.86 and effect the necessary adjustments
to reclassify completed projects into their appropriate PPE accounts while
recognizing the corresponding depreciation for each completed project.

We recommended and Management agreed that the CAO review all items
lodged in the PPE account, filter tangible items below ₱50,000.00 to be
accounted for as semi-expendable property, and thereafter effect necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts.

Further, we recommended and Management agreed to institutionalize the
implementation of internal control mechanisms for the semi-expendable
property provided for in COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024,
and for the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to prepare the necessary
forms, registry, and reports prescribed by the same Circular.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the City Accountant, in coordination with the Barangay Office
Affairs (BAO) and the Liga ng mga Barangay, to obtain documents of
ownership of properties and conduct a thorough examination of the
ownership status of the land on which the subject infrastructure assets
are situated, particularly those identified in Appendix 8;
b. Upon verification of ownership, consider facilitating the necessary steps
to transfer the identified assets directly utilized by the respective
barangays through donation, in accordance with Section 381 of RA No.
7160 and other relevant provisions, to ensure that the individual in actual
physical possession thereof is held responsible for their proper use,
maintenance, and accountability;
c. Require the City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries
to reclassify items, totaling ₱4,655,979.35, that do not meet the asset
recognition for PPE, pursuant to IPSAS 17 and Section 4.2 of COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to present fairly the
affected accounts in the financial statements; and
d. Require the City Accountant, through BAO, to ensure that the necessary
adjustments are also reflected in the barangay’s books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to require:
a. The Inventory Committee and the CGSO to prioritize the reconciliation of the
RPCPPE with the accounting records;
b. The City Agriculturist to conduct an inventory of biological assets and render
a report thereon, including the fair market value of each stock, to the City
Accountant for proper recording in the books of accounts and to henceforth,
keep a systematic record of births, deaths, and disposal of breeding stocks. In
case of death or disposal, he shall facilitate the request for relief of
accountability in accordance with Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No.
92-386 dated October 20, 1992, for dropping the account from the books; and
c. The City Accounting Office to maintain and update the WOABSLC monthly
using the reports submitted by the City Agriculturist and require, as well, the
CGSO to maintain the WOABSPC as a reference for identifying each animal
in the conduct of physical inventory as well as for reconciliation with accounting
records.

We recommended and Management agreed to enhance the coordination and
communication between the City Legal Office (CLO), City Assessor’s Office,
City General Services Office (CGSO) and CAO to expedite property
valuation and documentation process by establishing a tracking system or
timeline for property documentation. A focal person from each office could be
designated to oversee the timely submission and follow-up on any missing
documents.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRM Officer), City Engineer, and
City Accountant designate personnel to oversee the verification of
completion of the 26 projects under the LDRRMF CA – CO of CYs 2016-
2019, through comprehensive review and retrieval of relevant documents to
facilitate a timely liquidation process. Furthermore, we recommended that,
upon project completion and proper liquidation, the City Accountant make the
necessary accounting entries to transfer the assets to the GF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the CAO should see to it
that disbursements for payment of salaries and wages of casual and job order
personnel are completely supported with proper documents, as prescribed
under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, before payments are
made to avoid issuance of Notices of Suspension.

We recommended and Management agreed that the CDRRM Officer, City
Engineer, and City Accountant assign personnel to oversee the verification
of project completion for programs and projects under the STF, through a
comprehensive review and retrieval of relevant documents to facilitate the
timely liquidation process and ensure accurate recording of completed
projects.

We further recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant
revert the unutilized funds, specifically those transferred from 2010 to 2017,
to the GF, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September
12, 2012, for other social services as may be authorized by the local
sanggunian.

1.10. We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. Require the Inventory Committee to fully implement the one-time
cleansing of PPE account balances per COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020, to facilitate the reconciliation of year-end PPE balances;
and

b. Direct the CAO and CGSO to designate focal persons for each office who
will diligently reconcile their records and subsequently make necessary
adjustments to their respective property records.

2.14. We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The CEO expedite the preparation of the Fuel Consumption Reports,
Report of Completed Projects, and the COCs and regularly provide the
CAO with copies thereof monthly; and

b. The CAO assign personnel to focus on verifying the items for identification
included in the CIP account and to effect the necessary adjustments for
reclassifying these projects into their appropriate PPE accounts while
recognizing the corresponding depreciation and accumulated
depreciation for each completed project.

3.13. We recommended and Management agreed that the City Government carry
out the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020, to ensure that the PPE balances are accurate and that the
affected accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements.

4.10. We recommended and Management agreed to conduct a thorough
operational review of the Slaughterhouse, Market, and Septage Operations
to pinpoint the root causes of financial losses and implement corrective
actions to enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of operations and avoid
continuous financial losses.

5.7. We recommended and the City Engineer and concerned officials agreed to
regularly monitor and assess the implementation of projects under HFEP
and other projects to ensure efficient, economical, and effective project
implementation.

6.8. We recommended and the CEO and the CGSO agreed to fast-track the
necessary documentation for the full liquidation of the said projects so that
the unutilized balance of ₱399,240.18 of the financial assistance to LGUs will
be reverted to the National Treasury in compliance with Section 3.8 of LBC
No. 140 dated December 28, 2021, to allow the National Government to
utilize the same for other PPAs.

7.7. We recommended and Management agreed that the City Government remit
to the National Treasury the unutilized BGCM funds amounting to
₱692,738.42 in compliance with Section 3.9 of Local Budget Circular No. 125
dated April 7, 2020, to allow the National Government to utilize the same for
other useful programs or projects.

8.11. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Assessor to
prepare the city's updated SMVs pursuant to Section 212 of the LGC and
eventually draft an Ordinance for consideration of the Sanggunian,
incorporating the proposed SMVs, assessment level, and tax rate.

9.13. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct all heads of offices to
require their drivers to properly accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets,
Monthly Report of Official Travels, and Report of Fuel Consumption to
ensure that all items are filled out so that the reasonableness of fuel consumed
for the period can be determined. Also, for the stationary and construction
and heavy equipment, the parameters in fuel consumption usage, i.e.,
number of hours used and rate of usage of equipment, should be provided in
the report.

9.14. We also recommended and the CGSO agreed to endeavor to submit the
monthly reports on time, supported by the driver’s trip tickets.

9.15. Further, we recommended and the CGSO also agreed to repair or replace
defective odometers.

10.12. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct all heads of offices to
submit purchase requisitions for supplies, materials, and equipment
necessary for the day-to-day operations or in the pursuit of the principal
mandate of the agency in accordance with the schedule in the APP or as may
be preferred by the CGSO (i.e. monthly, quarterly), for consolidated
procurement by the latter.

11.10. We recommended and Management agreed to consider the construction of a
suitable garage for the safekeeping of government vehicles to protect them
from the elements and safeguard them against damage and possible theft or
unauthorized cannibalization of parts in accordance with Section V(5) of
COA Circular No. 75-6 dated November 7, 1975.

12.7. We recommended and the City Treasurer’s Office (CTO) agreed to
communicate with the depository bank regarding the steps to replace the 913
obsolete checks they currently possess. We further recommended that the
CTO coordinate with all depository banks that hold checking accounts for the
City, before the implementation of the new Check Design Standards, to
ensure all bank checks are successfully replaced.

For the PDAF utilized for intelligence purposes, we recommended for the immediate refund of the amount of ₱500,000.00 so that these could be used instead for bona fide projects and programs of the government. Discussion was done by top Management that the amount of ₱500,000.00 will be restituted from the General Fund- Unappropriated Surplus. The Notice of Disallowance (ND) is on appeal.
observations.managementAction: Settlement had been done in July 2012; the liquidation documents were sent to COA Central Office for audit and action. The ND is on appeal. The City Accounting Office had been following up on the matter.
observations.reason:
- Awaiting the COA Central Office audit and action.
- The ND is on appeal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and:
a) Management agreed to see to it that the guidelines on PCF are strictly
observed; and
b) The Petty Cash Custodians prepare and secure all the necessary
documentary requirements to support payments out of the PCF.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to use a checklist to ensure
that all documentary requirements for the replenishment of petty cash fund are
complied with before certifying the completeness of the supporting documents.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to:
a. Assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required
code format in the property identification system as provided in Section 5 of
the Circular;
b. Prepare a PIP and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten
calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities
pursuant to Section 5 of the Circular; and
c. Record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major
account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the
physical count.

We further recommended and the Property Unit agreed to prepare a List of
PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing
PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to
the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish
the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to enjoin the concerned
department heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on
the local roads asset management system and earnestly perform their respective
duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October
23, 2015.

We further recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to conduct an
annual physical count of all LRN and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to
be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting unit not later than January 31 of each
year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the
necessary adjusting entries are prepared before the statement date.

We recommended and Management agreed to require:
a. The Inventory Committee and the CGSO to prioritize the reconciliation of
the RPCPPE with the accounting records;
b. The City Agriculturist to conduct an inventory of biological assets and
render a report thereon including the fair market value of each stock to the
City Accountant, for proper recording in the books of accounts and to
henceforth, keep a systematic record of births, deaths, and disposal of
breeding stocks. In case of death or disposal, he shall facilitate the request
for relief of accountability in accordance with Sections 151 and 152 of COA
Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, for dropping the account from
the books; and
c. The City Accounting Office to maintain and update the WOABSLC monthly
using the reports submitted by the City Agriculturist, and require, as well,
the CGSO to maintain the WOABSPC as reference for identifying each
animal in the conduct of physical inventory as well as for reconciliation with
accounting records.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to designate one of its
personnel to regularly monitor and demand from the City Engineer the liquidation
reports of LGSF funded projects and to draw a journal voucher to take up the
liquidation in order to present fairly the account Due from Other NGAS and the
related asset and expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the personnel-in-charge
of posting Trust Fund reports to include the LGSF Report on Fund Utilization and
Status of Program/Project Implementation in posting to City’s website and in at least
three (3) conspicuous places in the premises of the City pursuant to Local Budget
Circular Nos. 117, 119 and 122 to fully achieve accountability and transparency in
the implementation and utilization of LGSF.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary
adjustments/and or corollary entries to take up the appropriate expense and revenue
accounts, including Other Deferred Credits for ongoing capital outlays, so that the
balances of these accounts may be fairly presented in the financial statements. We
further recommend that Management determine whether the purpose for the trust
receipt was already fulfilled or the project completed so that the corresponding
revenue may be taken out from the Other Deferred Credits account and the assets
transferred to the General Fund. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance to the IPSAS
and the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended and the City Government agreed to conduct an annual
evaluation of the performance/quality of City-Wide CCTV Services of PLDT, Inc.,
the service provider, to determine whether the services rendered were satisfactory
enough for it to be allowed to continue its services in the succeeding year(s).

We recommended that the City Accounting Office and City General Service
Office reconcile the excess payment amounting to ₱47,801.78 for the billing period
May 15, 2020, to May 16, 2020.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to add a disclosure in the
Notes to Financial Statements regarding the lease of Dumaguete City-Wide Fiber
Optic Network and CCTV provided by PLDT, Inc. to keep the users of the financial
statements informed of the existence and nature of such lease transaction.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to submit the lacking
documents to the Audit Team for verification and to, subsequently, ensure that all
documentary requirements for the procurement of infrastructure projects are
complied with before certifying the completeness of the supporting documents.

We recommended that the CDRRMO coordinate with the OCD on the
preparation and submission of the City’s PSCP pursuant to NDRRMC Memorandum
No 33, s. 2018.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) institutionalize
the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the following activities:
a) To conduct a risk assessment, risk and impact analysis;
b) To identify critical processes and functions;
c) To determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operations;
d) To formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical processes
and functions and its related steps to be followed to eliminate, if not
mitigate, the impact of the determined disruptions following the prescribed
templates and requirements in the PSCP guidebook; and
e) To submit a plan to the CDRRMO for review, to the Local Chief Executive
for approval, and to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the passage of a
resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of accepting
20% DF fund transfers from the barangays for their implementation and instead
ensure that the City Engineer’s Office implement the City’s annual expected projects.
We also recommended and Management agreed to direct the Barangays to be
responsible for the efficient and effective implementation of their development
plans, programs, objectives and priorities, being local government units distinct from
the City.

Accordingly, the MCW has not been fully implemented in the City.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions
of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA, particularly on the setting
up and maintenance of GAD database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive
planning, programming and policy formulation; setting up of GAD Agenda; and
formulation of GAD Codes or Ordinances that will support the City’s efforts in
promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, economic empowerment
and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and
women’s empowerment.

We recommended that the City Accountant conduct periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and the City Mayor to file the request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts to the Audit Team Leader supported by the documents enumerated under Item 8.3 (a) and (b) of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office had already sent demand letters to concerned employees who are still active in service. As regards to the deceased accountable officers/employees, they had been securing the respective death certificates as one of the supporting documents for the request for write-off. They also conducted quarterly verification and analysis of the receivables.
observations.reason: - Unavailability of some supporting documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require: (a) the Inventory Committee to conduct an actual count of inventories and prepare the RPCI; (b) the CGSO and the City Accountant to maintain property records and reconcile both records; (c) the requisitioning office to prepare and submit the RIS to the GSO on a weekly basis; and (d) the CGSO to consolidate on the SSMI all RIS and submit the same to the Accountant weekly, as basis for recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accounting Office already implemented the practice of recording the issuance of supplies and materials during the JEV preparation as long as SSMI’s were attached to each disbursement voucher on process. The City Accounting Office already maintained the Subsidiary Ledger Card for each Inventory account and monitoring is done as well.
The consolidation of the RIS from other offices using the SSMI is 90% complete.
observations.reason:
- The delay was due to the lack of manpower in view of the retirement of many GSO personnel.
- Needs verification of prior years’ balances.
Also, the City Accounting Office already maintained the Subsidiary Ledger Card for each Inventory account and monitoring is done as well.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and City General Services Officer reconcile their records with the RPCPPE and thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments in their respective property records.
observations.managementAction:
The CGSO and City Accounting Office has an ongoing reconciliation of property records. The CGSO conducted physical count of PPE annually.
- Incomplete details of the PPE Account balances prior to year 2008.
The absence of supporting documents prior to year 2008 posed difficulty in the reconciliation.
observations.reason:
- The delay was due to the lack of manpower in view of the retirement of many GSO personnel.
- Incomplete details of the PPE Account balances prior to year 2008.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare and furnish the Auditor a copy of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported by the following: (a) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; and (b) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries. We further recommended that Management likewise delegate the receipt and acknowledgement of the donations in-kind to the CGSO which will take responsibility of the basic controls in the receipt, recording, inventory, storage and distribution of all donations in-kind received, and will prepare and submit the required reports together with the supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Discussion among the concerned offices on the formulation of system and procedures concerning receiving donations in-kind and the CGSO and Accounting Office. List of donations were partially submitted to Accounting Office.
observations.reason:
- Need coordination of the concerned offices
- The submission of the required necessary information were not properly complied with, however, the CSWDO submitted a letter justifying the non-submission of the subject documents yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require copies of duly signed distribution lists showing the full names of recipient frontline workers as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary information, including the kind and quantity of food items received in order to validate actual distribution and to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: The submission of the required distribution lists and necessary information were not properly complied with, however, the CSWDO submitted a letter justifying the non-submission of the subject documents yet.
observations.reason: - Incomplete documentation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant together with the heads of other concerned offices submit the following documents to the Auditor to determine compliance with the conditions on the grant of COVID-19 Hazard Pay set forth in Paragraph 4 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-01 dated March 24, 2020, to wit: a) Authority from the head of agency/office directing the personnel to render service during the period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well as the entitlement of the COVID-19 hazard pay; and b) Certification by the City Accountant that personnel who were granted COVID-19 Hazard Pay had not been paid with any other type of hazard pay under existing laws.
observations.managementAction: The retrieval of the relevant documents as bases in the preparation of the office order and certification is ongoing.
observations.reason: - Retrieval of supporting documents requires enough time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Establish a comprehensive database of low-income families to facilitate identification and validation of target beneficiaries for social work, disaster risk reduction management and other similar initiatives of the City; and (b) Strictly adhere to the requirements prescribed under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09, series of 2020, in the implementation of the SAP and other similar programs of DSWD to ensure that financial assistance are given to the intended beneficiaries and the objectives of the program are attained.
observations.managementAction: The establishment of Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) is ongoing. The CSWDO has only the profiles of the clients being served.
observations.reason: - Awaiting completion of the establishment of CBMS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create a system that would facilitate identification and validation of qualified target beneficiaries to ensure submission of the required liquidation reports within the prescribed period as stipulated in the MOA. The unused funds of P23,592,000.00 should be returned to DSWD RO VII per MOA if not liquidated within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The unused funds of P23,592,000.00 was returned to DSWD RO VII on January 11, 2021 through Check No. 1334252.
observations.reason: - Awaiting completion of CBMS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant deduct ten percent (10%) from every progress payment for infrastructure projects undertaken by contract, as retention money up to fifty percent (50%) completion of the works if the project is within schedule, otherwise, it should be continuously imposed, in accordance with the pertinent provision of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Retention money for infrastructure projects undertaken by contracts were already deducted from every progress payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team for verification, and to ensure that all documentary requirements for the procurement of infrastructure projects are complied with before certifying as to the completeness of the supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was noted and a letter was forwarded to GSO by the Accounting Office on June 28, 2021 informing them to submit the necessary supporting documents relating to this as part of their responsibility.
observations.reason:
- Lack of manpower at the implementing offices.
- The submission of the required supporting documents is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the personnel in charge of the requisitioning office to properly and completely fill up the relevant and required information in the JOs/POs to allow comparison with the prices offered by other suppliers or with the prevailing market prices in the locality, to determine the price reasonableness of the items procured.
observations.managementAction: Travel orders were requested from the Provincial Governor to support the travels outside the province. (Note: This appears mismatched; management indicated actions to ensure proper documentation in JOs/POs.)
observations.reason:
- Need to retrieve related files/records
- The accounts of other organizations remained dormant because most of them were already dissolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure compliance with Section 3.5 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020, otherwise, they will be held answerable for negligence in case of defects on the goods delivered.
observations.managementAction: Purchase Orders prepared during the year already contained the original warranty clause.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat comply with the posting requirements in the GPPB online portal, website of the LGU or at any conspicuous place in the premises of the LGU pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat had already complied with the posting requirements at the LGU website. The preparation of the Procurement Monitoring Reports (PMRs) for submission to GPPB is already 60-70% complete.
observations.reason: - Need to complete the posting requirements at GPPB online portal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management expedite the implementation of all projects, programs and activities (PPAs) under the LDRRMF by addressing the causes of delay, like insufficient funds, issues on road right-of-way, lack of POW, and requiring the barangays to implement their projects. We also recommended strict adherence to the provisions of Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: All concerned officers were already informed on this matter. Preparation of the required supporting documents is ongoing.
observations.reason: - Awaiting the completion of supporting documents (especially those relating to the fuel component of the projects).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CEO provide the CAO with copies of the report on completed projects on at least a quarterly basis, together with the certificates of completion. Likewise, we recommended that the CAO assign personnel to focus on the verification of the items for identification in order to effect the necessary adjustments and meantime, disclose the same in the Notes to the Financial Statements. We further recommended that the CAO verify the CIP accounts against available records and henceforth, maintain complete subsidiary ledgers to support the balance of the accounts appearing in the general ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The CEO had partially provided copies of certificates of completion. The City Accounting Office is in the process of reconciling the items for identification based on the available records for appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.reason:
- Unavailability of records for those accounts pertaining to the previous years hamper the reconciliation.
- Identification of depreciable assets purchased from CY 2002 to 2007 is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant regularly monitor and demand submission of liquidation of funds transferred to other LGUs, and thereafter, draw a journal voucher to take up the expenses in order to present fairly the Due from LGUs and the related expense accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring of the liquidation of the fund releases to the barangays had been conducted by the Accounting Office and the reconciliation of previous years’ balances is ongoing. Demand letters were also sent to concerned barangays for the immediate settlement/liquidation of their accounts with the City.
observations.reason:
- Unavailability of records for the prior years’ balances.
- Part of the mitigation done by the Accounting Office was the non-processing of fund transfer if prior transfers were not yet settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management assign or designate relevant officials who will be responsible for the conduct of a feasibility study and a five-year BP for the operation of the septage facility in accordance with the procedures provided in Items 4.2 and 4.6 of the Manual.
observations.managementAction:
A feasibility study was already prepared, subject for review and approval. The Office of the City Economic Enterprises had already assigned Mrs. Stephanie C. Cassion responsible for Market, Slaughterhouse and Transport Terminal to prepare the Five-Year BP and FSSE reports, while Engr. Eula Escober is responsible for the Septage Treatment Facility to prepare the same.
- Time constraints and lack of personnel to handle the job as the response to COVID-19 pandemic was prioritized.
observations.reason:
- Time constraints and lack of personnel to handle the job as the response to COVID-19 pandemic was prioritized.
- The draft feasibility study is still subject for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide additional personnel in the Office of the BAC to assist in the preparation of reports, especially the PMR to be approved by the LCE and submitted to the GPPB within 14 days after the end of each semester. We further recommended that the BAC Secretariat post all the NOAs and NTPs in the PhilGEPS pursuant to Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: One personnel had been added to the BAC Secretariat already. Posting requirements for Notices of Award (NOAs) and Notices to Proceed (NTPs) had been complied with. The preparation of the Procurement Monitoring Reports (PMRs) for submission to GPPB is already 60-70% complete.
observations.reason:
- The recommendation was noted. The preparation of the PMRs is ongoing.
- Time constraints and lack of manpower caused the delay of the implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Council through the LDRRM Officer in coordination with CSWDO, CHO, CPDO, CEO and other concerned offices comply with the standards provided in the Disaster Preparedness Minimum Standards, Volume 2.
observations.managementAction: The compliance with Minimum Standards on Disaster Preparedness is ongoing. The construction of the ₱35M Evacuation Center and Multi-purpose Building, designed as a disaster-resilient facility in Barangay Batinguel, procurement of required equipage and various capacity building trainings were among the PPAs being prioritized during the year.
observations.reason: - The full compliance takes time complying with the procurement procedures and availability of personnel to do the required tasks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City hire qualified personnel to occupy the positions of the staff of the LDRRMO in accordance with the position titles, qualification standards and salary grades set under Section 6.0 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: One staff was hired during the year and the hiring of additional personnel is ongoing.
observations.reason:
- Need to comply with the hiring of personnel procedures, timeline and approval.
- Personnel hiring is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS thoroughly review the GPB, paying particular attention on the output performance targets of PPAs that should be doable within the year in accordance with Section 8.2 of the PCW-DED A and DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
observations.managementAction: The GPB was already being reviewed by the GFPS through series of coordination meetings. The implementation of various PPAs were also being monitored.
observations.reason:
- Need to address GAD PPAs implementation issues and concerns during the year.
- Need to capacitate more the various departments/units in the implementation of GAD PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and effect adjusting entries to reclassify the erroneous accounts to their proper asset accounts and to take up Prior Period Adjustment for the expenses incurred in CY 2016 in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs per PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already made partial adjusting entries regarding this matter.
observations.reason: - Some accounts need verification before being considered for adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to avail of the remedies provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160 to enforce collection of delinquent taxes and fill up of levy vacancies, especially for core positions at the CTO to augment its manpower requirement.
observations.managementAction: The CTO personnel assigned at RPT Division continually sent RPT billings and issued warrants to taxpayers. The CTO manpower requirements were also being addressed during the year.
observations.reason:
- Necessary steps leading to auction sale was suspended due to DOF Department Circular suspending implementation due to COVID-19 pandemic.
- Reasons of uncollected RPT included: owners of properties declared as private road lots refusing to pay; properties under court litigation; deceased owners with unknown heirs; and increase in RPT delinquency due to pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management deduct from the salaries and other benefits accruing to its employees authorized and other deductions which in no case shall reduce their monthly net take home pay to an amount prescribed under the existing law.
observations.managementAction: The HR Office had been steadily coherent in such implementation thus, percentage of employees/borrowers with below the 5K NTHP threshold gradually reduced.
observations.reason: - Some employees were already bound to the terms and conditions of the financial institutions from where they borrowed funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management designate a responsible employee to prepare a Five-Year BP and FSSE report for each LEE in accordance with the LEE Manual as basis for the LCE and Sangguniang Panlungsod in coming up with a decision on whether to continue the LEE operations or other related actions to make them financially self-sufficient and eventually profitable to ensure the efficient and effective provision of basic services and facilities to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Economic Enterprises had already assigned Mrs. Stephanie C. Cassion to prepare the Five-year BP and FSSE reports for Market, Slaughterhouse and Transport Terminal. On the other hand, Engr. Eula Escober was assigned for the Septage Treatment Facility to prepare the same.
observations.reason:
- Awaiting for the final BP and FSSE reports.
- Time constraints and lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the CPDO and City Engineer to fast track the implementation of projects under the 20% DF by addressing the causes of delay, like insufficient funds, issues on road right-of-way, lack of POW, and requiring the barangays to implement their projects. We further recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, properly plan the projects for inclusion in the AIP and budget allocation, and closely monitor project implementation.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO and the City Engineer had already monitored the timely implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund. For CY 2021, the City appropriated ₱125,555,000.00 for the implementation of programs, projects and activities (PPAs) under the 20% DF.
observations.reason: - Need to address concerns of the delay of implementation like issues on road right-of-way, and preparation of POW and other necessary documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CEO cause the timely submission of the COCs and reports on disbursement for fuel and lubricants component of the various projects in order to fully liquidate the funds received and/or return any unutilized amount to the Source Agencies.
observations.managementAction: The CAO submitted quarterly reports and coordinated with the CEO to fast track the liquidation of funds. The submission of COCs and liquidation reports had been partially complied by the CEO. On the other hand, some borrowers provided proofs of payments for deposits made directly through the banks especially those of national grants.
observations.reason:
- Fuel, oil and lubricants consumption reports were not yet regularly submitted.
- The submission of COCs and recording is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant: (a) Review all outstanding payables, validate available supporting documents, and revert those which are not supported with valid claims and those which are aged two years or more; (b) Investigate accounts payable which have remained dormant and those with negative balances and determine the cause/s and prepare adjusting journal entries for all errors noted; and (c) Henceforth, record accounts payable in accordance with the regulations provided in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and maintain subsidiary ledgers indicating the name of creditor including the address to allow confirmation.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office reviewed and validated the Accounts Payable and adjustments were made to accounts aged more than two years per JEV No. 2018-07-0213. Various reconciling items with the DBP amounting to ₱392,470.60 were already adjusted in September 2018. The Accounting Office will henceforth record accounts payable in accordance with NGAS and maintain subsidiary ledgers indicating the name of creditor including the address to allow confirmation.
observations.reason:
- Verification of financial statements and its supporting schedules showed that payable account still included aged balances as well as negative balances. Some items are still for reconciliation with the available records.
- Unavailability of records for those accounts pertaining to the previous years hamper the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to: (a) require the immediate liquidation of cash advances by sending out another demand letters signed both by the City Accountant and by the City Mayor or his authorized representative; (b) verify and analyze the negative balances noted in the Due from Officers and Employees and effect the necessary adjustments; and (c) request write-off for long outstanding cash advances, especially of those who are deceased pursuant of the guidelines/procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The CAO reviewed and validated the cash advances and adjustments were made where applicable. Supporting documents like death certificates for deceased accountable officers were being secured. The Accounting Office is in the process of reconciliation for prior years’ balances.
observations.reason:
- Some required documents for the request for write-off, like death certificates of accountable officers were already secured by the Accounting Office.
- The retrieval of supporting documents requires enough time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant review and analyze the list of outstanding checks and regularly draw a JEV for the cancellation of stale checks to reflect the correct monthly balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments amounting to ₱5,038.00 were partially taken up and suppliers/creditors were informed about the stale checks. Part of the reconciling items amounting to ₱392,470.60 were adjusted in September 2018.
observations.reason:
- Some checks were still in the hands of suppliers/creditors.
- Difficulty in retrieving supporting documents, especially those pertaining to prior years’ transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant: (a) verify the reconciling items and draw a JEV to adjust all valid reconciling items pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; (b) conduct a thorough verification of the dormant bank accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments; and (c) provide copies of the bank reconciliation statements to the depository banks so that any discrepancies noted between the records of the banks and of the City can be immediately resolved.
observations.managementAction: Various reconciling items with the DBP amounting to ₱392,470.60 were already adjusted in September 2018. Adjustments were made where supporting documents were available. The City Accounting Office provided bank reconciliation statements to the depository banks for clarification.
observations.reason: - Difficulty in retrieving the supporting documents, especially those pertaining to prior years’ transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management expedite the implementation of all projects, programs and activities (PPAs) under the LDRRMF to strengthen the capacity of the City Government to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects of disasters or calamity.
observations.managementAction: The Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer already assigned a staff to monitor all PPAs under the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: - Due to lack of manpower in the CDRRMO, the implementation of LDRRM PPAs were sometimes not given priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003. In the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame; (b) Enforce strictly the City’s projects and programs relative to solid waste management and ensure 100% compliance thereof; and (c) Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: On March 30, 2021, the open dumpsite was finally closed and the rehabilitation of the closed dumpsite is ongoing. The Central Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) in Barangay Camanjac was constructed. The preparation for the full operation of the pyrolysis machine for recycling wastes like installation of electricity was ongoing. The CENRO conducted massive IEC activities in partnership with the barangays. Series of meetings, discussions and lectures on solid waste management were also conducted. Robust implementation of ordinances on solid waste management was also done during the year.
observations.reason: - Due to the scale of work, full implementation of the sanitary landfill and related infrastructure requires time and resources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management order the SDO personnel to desist from receiving loan payments for loan amortization from people’s organizations and to instruct all borrowers to directly pay to the CTO. We also recommended that the City Treasurer prepare a separate report of collections together with duplicate copies of the official receipt to the Accounting Office for posting in the individual subsidiary ledgers of the borrowers. We further recommended that the City Accountant immediately send demand letters to debtors to ascertain collectability of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Sectoral Desk Office stopped receiving loan payments from People’s Organization (POs). The CAO in coordination with CTO sent demand and collection letters to various POs. Four organizations had already fully paid their accounts. The City Accounting Office had been monitoring the fund releases to the barangays. For the barangay RPT shares as of December 31, 2021, it was already processed on January 5, 2022.
observations.reason:
- The accounts of other organizations remained dormant because most of them were already dissolved.
- Both records of the CAO and GSO are still for reconciliation due to incomplete records and supporting documents.
- Need to retrieve related documentation took time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the DSWD to observe existing policy on program monitoring activities to immediately address any problem, ensure loan amortization payment in accordance with the loan payment schedule and achieve program objective. We also recommended that the City Accountant send demand letters to SKAs to settle their monthly/quarterly/semi-annually rollback of the seed capital assistance.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent to various organizations and individuals concerned. On the other hand, some borrowers provided proofs of payments for deposits made directly through the banks especially those of national grants.
observations.reason: - The addresses of the members of dissolved organizations are unknown, hence the delay in sending out demand letters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management order the conduct of complete physical count of PPEs as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21 dated February 17, 2016 and other existing regulations. Results of the physical inventory should be reconciled with the accounting and property records to present accurate balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office conducted an inventory of all real and movable properties as of June 30, 2016. The CAO and GSO are still for reconciliation due to incomplete records and supporting documents.
observations.reason: - Both records of the CAO and GSO are still for reconciliation due to incomplete records and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit travel orders for his travels listed in Annex A to the audit team and henceforth, secure permission from the Provincial Governor every time he travels outside the Province of Negros Oriental pursuant to Section 96(b) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Travel orders were requested from the Provincial Governor to support the travels outside the province. The request had not been acted upon yet.
observations.reason: - The request to the Provincial Governor had not been acted upon yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Require the City Legal Officer in coordination with the City General Services Officer to initiate the titling of the real properties to avoid possible ownership disputes in the future and to enable the Accounting Office to record their cost in the books of accounts; (b) Instruct the City Accountant to ensure that all recorded transactions have been adequately supported by valid documents and to maintain subsidiary ledgers to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and (c) Require the General Services Office to maintain complete records and documents of all the properties of the City Government pursuant to Section 490 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The CLO went over all files of lot acquisitions from the 30 barangays, and identified which among these had already been issued Certificates Authorizing Registration (CARs) by the BIR. To date, more than 90 transactions have CARs already. However, a lot of these have to be updated since the BIR now requires the electronic CAR (or e-CAR). Hence, the CLO will look into this in greater detail. Once the e-CARs are procured, the CLO can then continue the individual titling of these lots in favor of the City of Dumaguete. After which, the CLO would provide copies of the TCTs to the City Accounting and the General Services Office for proper recording of their cost in the books of accounts. The GSO’s list is based on the TCTs and /or OCTs forwarded by the CLO after the processing and the transfer of titles to the city government. The CGSO hold in its custody the TCTs forwarded by the City Legal Office. Also, the GSO had been forwarding all deed of conveyances to the City Accountant’s Office for the valuation of the land account.
- Recent BIR requirements/developments in processing of lot titles had to be complied with.
- The CLO will continue the individual titling once e-CARs are procured.
observations.reason:
- Some records need verification and further action.
- Recent BIR requirements (e-CAR) necessitate updating and further work before completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the City Accountant, Budget Officer, City Treasurer and all other officials responsible for the improper charges against the 5% LDRRMF to refund the amount totaling ₱1,125,112.85 and henceforth, to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 in the use of the 5% LDRRMF to avoid audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant’s Office sought the assistance of the CLO on this regard. The CAO and CTO recommended to Management to formally initiate the restitution of the amount of ₱1,125,112.85 from the General Fund to LDRRMF Fund in order to replenish or refund the 5% LDRRMF for the subject purchases. It was proposed that the City Council pass a special appropriation ordinance for this matter.
observations.reason: - The proposed restitution required Management approval and the passage of a special appropriation ordinance. The process is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management desist from disbursing the unexpended PDAF balance of ₱1,941,164.20 and immediately return the total amount to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Management suspended all disbursements charged against PDAF and already returned portions of the unexpended balances in the total amount of ₱1,070,432.28 (₱935,886.84 for 2015-2018 and ₱134,545.44 for 2019). The Accounting personnel reconciled and prepared liquidation reports for the 15 PDAF projects. Some unreturned portions related to pending submission of certificates of completion.
observations.reason: - Not all unexpended portions of the unexpended balance were returned to the Bureau of Treasury. Some items pending due to submission of certificates of completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fully and immediately comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to promote transparency and accountability, to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern, and to avoid the penalty clause therein and always review the report and check the signboards before putting them up to ensure the correctness of all information thereon.
observations.managementAction: Required publication of all ongoing projects had been complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately undertake the recommendations of the Technical Working Group to make the necessary adjustments of the room volume to provide adequate insulation. Likewise, undertake all necessary measures, if still possible to make the storage facility operational/functional. Otherwise, determine the official/staff responsible/liable and impose appropriate administrative sanction.
observations.managementAction: The implementation for improvement was turned over to the City Engineer’s Office after a request of the ₱650,000.00 budget as per supplier’s quotation has been approved. However, procurement fails when the quotation exceeds the ABC. Program of Work will be submitted to CEO for the revision of the estimated total cost.
observations.reason: - The cost of the job order as per supplier’s quotation exceeded the budget. Procurement adjustments and program of work revision needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately require the City Accountant to give priority to and intensify the review and analysis of the reconciling items and accounts, and regularly prepare bank reconciliation statements; and to reconcile bank statements and book balances and clear reconciling items promptly.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of various bank accounts had been prioritized. In December 2016, bank reconciling items amounting to ₱1,166,381.42 were already reconciled and cleared both cash in bank and in books. Additional reconciling items were cleared in the amount of ₱392,470.60 in September 2018. The Accounting Office had been monitoring and providing updates.
observations.reason: - Retrieval of supporting documents took time and some documents for prior years were unavailable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to give priority to and intensify the review and analysis of the reconciling items and adjust those bank accounts which have not been reconciled, and coordinate with the depository banks to resolve discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office had been reviewing and validating reconciliations. Various reconciling items with the depository bank were adjusted. Reconciliation took some time because the CAO had to work back from prior years’ records and some documents were unavailable.
observations.reason:
- Difficulty in gathering the supporting documents to determine the correctness of the prior year balances.
- Reconciliation took some time because the CAO had to work back from prior years’ records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant analyze and validate the dormant accounts and accounts for “ID” classified under Due from NGOs/POs and the unsupported balances reflected in the subsidiary ledgers. If analysis/review of the accounts is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances from the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: Identification of dormant accounts is ongoing. The City Accounting Office had been monitoring the fund releases to the barangays. Demand letters were sent to debtors. Some organizations provided proofs of payments or settled their accounts.
observations.reason:
- Overstatement, misclassification and incomplete documentation persisted.
- Lack of supporting documents particularly transactions which occurred in the prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: (a) Immediately submit the audited Statement of Liquidation to the various source agencies for proper reporting and accounting of the funds received and utilized and to return any unutilized amount pursuant to the terms and conditions of the MOA and COA Circular No. 94-013; and (b) Monitor regularly the status/liquidation of fund transfers received from NGAs based on duly documented transactions and to keep a separate and complete file of documents/reports for each fund received.
observations.managementAction: The CAO submitted quarterly reports and coordinated with the CEO to fast track the liquidation of funds. Reconciliation and liquidation reports were being prepared. Some returned unspent balances were processed. Work on reconciling the Due to Other NGAs continues.
observations.reason:
- Lack of certificates of completion and clearance from the City Mayor to return the balance to the source agency.
- Some supporting documents were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Conduct review and reconciliation of all Due to Other NGAs accounts by ascertaining the status of project/activities completion, balances and status of liquidation; and (b) Require the City Accountant to likewise verify and investigate the status of the funds by confirming the balances with the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Update, review and reconciliation of balances in the subsidiary ledgers is ongoing. The Accounting Office is coordinating with source agencies to confirm balances where applicable.
observations.reason:
- Update, review and reconciliation of balances in the subsidiary ledgers is ongoing.
- The subsidiary ledgers of this account were not updated due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately release the RPT shares due to the barangays. Henceforth, adherence to Section 272 (d) of the Local Government Code is encouraged and require the City Accountant to determine the actual status of the 50% share of the community taxes and if found to be erroneously recorded, cause the transfer of the amount to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to be used for other barangay activities/programs.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office had been monitoring the fund releases to the barangays. For the barangay RPT shares as of December 31, 2021, it was already processed on January 5, 2022. The prior year balances amounting to ₱512,614.98 representing the RPT and community tax share remained dormant from 2006 up to the present. Reconciliation is ongoing to determine the correctness of the account.
observations.reason:
- Difficulty in gathering supporting documents to determine the correctness of the prior year balances.
- The prior year balances remained dormant and require reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to determine the composition of the amount set up as Items In Transit converted from the in-process accounts in 2002 as well as the payments or liquidations thereof, and to adjust the Items in Transit account in order to fairly present the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: There is an ongoing reconciliation to determine the composition of the amount set up during conversion in CY 2002. The accounting office is working on retrieval of old documents and reconciling prior year balances.
observations.reason:
- Reconciliation took some time because the CAO had to work back from the prior years’ records.
- Incomplete documents caused the delay in the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to request from the GSO for a list of assets acquired from 2002 to 2007, to compute the depreciation expense for all depreciable assets for the current audit period as well as from 2002 up to 2006 using the Prior Years’ Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation accounts in order to fairly present the asset and equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation expenses were already recognized for fixed assets acquired from 2008 up to the present. Identification of depreciable assets purchased from CY 2002 to 2007 is ongoing.
observations.reason:
- Lack of available documents for prior years’ balances to support some adjustments in the books of accounts.
- Identification of depreciable assets purchased from CY 2002 to 2007 is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider revising the rental rates of stall spaces in the public market to make it a viable enterprise in the process, take into consideration the volume of trade or business in each section in order to come up with more equitable and just rates, and avoid charging to market operations the salaries and other emoluments of personnel who are not actually rendering services therein.
observations.managementAction: In January 2011, the market management implemented an ordinance imposing a 25% adjustment on all new stall awardees and 5% increase every three years thereafter on the rental rates of the stall spaces at the Dumaguete City Public Market Complex.
observations.reason: - Increase of rental rates had not been imposed to the old stallholders since 1988. As embodied in the ordinance, the rate increase applies only to the new stall awardees after the enactment of said ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant identify the composition of the portion of government equity representing the costs of the different completed projects and transfer the same to the General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The assigned personnel in the City Accountant’s Office had been working on the liquidation of completed projects under Trust Fund with the implementing agencies. The CAO in coordination with CTO processed liquidations and reconciled trust fund records.
observations.reason: - Retrieval of supporting documents took time and some documents were unavailable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the City Accountant to effect the appropriate adjustments to reconcile accounts Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds is ongoing. The Accounting Office had to work back from prior years’ records to identify and adjust differences.
observations.reason:
- Reconciliation took some time because the CAO had to work back from the prior years’ records.
- Incomplete documents caused the delay in the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City desist from disbursing the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) balance of ₱1,941,164.20 and immediately return the total amount to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting personnel reconciled and prepared liquidation reports for the 15 PDAF projects. Management suspended all disbursements charged against PDAF and already returned portions of the total amount of ₱1,070,432.28 to the Bureau of Treasury pending submission of certificates of completion from the Implementing Agency and certification of undelivered or cancelled items in the case of unimplemented projects.
observations.reason: Not all unexpended balance were returned to the Bureau of Treasury pending submission of certificates of completion for the completed projects and clearance from the City Mayor to return the balance to the Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation: For the PDAF utilized for intelligence purposes, we recommended for the immediate refund of the amount of ₱500,000.00 so that these could be used instead for bona fide pro-poor projects and programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: Settlement had been done in July 2013. The Notice of Disallowance (ND) is on appeal. Management indicated that the amount of ₱500,000.00 will be restituted from the General Fund-Unappropriated Surplus.
observations.reason: Awaiting the COA Central Office audit and action and pending Management action on the restitution. The Notice of Disallowance is on appeal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to conduct periodic
verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables; and b) the
City Mayor to file the request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts to the
Audit Team Leader supported by the documents enumerated under Item 8.3 (a) and
(b) of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to require (a) the
Inventory Committee to conduct an actual count of inventories and prepare the
RPCI; (b) the CGSO and the City Accountant to maintain property records and
reconcile both records; (c) the requisitioning office to prepare and submit the RIS to
the GSO on a weekly basis; and (d) the CGSO to consolidate on the SSMI all RIS and
submit the same to the Accountant weekly, as basis for recording in the books of
accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation and the City Accountant and City General
Services Officer agreed to reconcile their records with the RPCPPE and thereafter,
effect the necessary adjustments in their respective property records.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare and furnish the
Auditor a copy of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances
supported by the following: (a) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind;
and (b) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries in accordance with
Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

We further recommended and Management likewise agreed to delegate the
receipt and acknowledgement of the donations in-kind to the CGSO which will take
responsibility of the basic controls in the receipt, recording, inventory, storage and
distribution of all donations in-kind received, and will prepare and submit the
required reports together with the supporting documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the City Social Welfare
and Development Office, City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and
other offices concerned to submit copies of duly signed distribution lists showing the
full names of recipient frontline workers as well as the date of receipt, and such other
necessary information, including the kind and quantity of food items received in
order to validate actual distribution and to establish accountability.

We recommended and the City Accountant together with the heads of other
concerned offices agreed to submit the following documents to the Auditor to
determine compliance with the conditions on the grant of COVID-19 Hazard Pay set
forth in Paragraph 4 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-01 dated March 24, 2020, to
wit:
a) Authority from the head of agency/office directing the personnel to render
service during the period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working
hours as well as the entitlement of the COVID-19 hazard pay; and
b) Certification by the City Accountant that personnel who were granted
COVID-19 Hazard Pay had not been paid with any other type of hazard pay
under existing laws.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
(a) Establish a comprehensive database of low-income families to facilitate
identification and validation of target beneficiaries for social work, disaster
risk reduction management and other similar initiatives of the City; and
(b) Strictly adhere to the requirements prescribed under DSWD
Memorandum Circular No. 09, series of 2020, in the implementation of the
SAP and other similar programs of DSWD to ensure that financial
assistance are given to the intended beneficiaries and the objectives of the
program are attained.

We recommended and Management agreed to create a system that would
facilitate identification and validation of qualified target beneficiaries to ensure
submission of the required liquidation reports within the prescribed period as
stipulated in the MOA.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to deduct ten percent
(10%) from every progress payment for infrastructure projects undertaken by
contract, as retention money up to fifty percent (50%) completion of the works if the
project is within schedule, otherwise, it should be continuously imposed, in accordance
with the pertinent provision of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to submit immediately the
lacking documents to the Audit Team for verification, and to ensure that all
documentary requirements for the procurement of infrastructure projects are
complied with before certifying as to the completeness of the supporting documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the personnel in charge
of the requisitioning office to properly and completely fill up the relevant and
required information in the JOs/POs to allow comparison with the prices offered by
other suppliers or with the prevailing market prices in the locality, to determine the
price reasonableness of the items procured.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure compliance with Section
3.5 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020, otherwise, they will be held
answerable for negligence in case of defects on the goods delivered.

We recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to comply with the
posting requirements in the GPPB online portal, website of the LGU or at any
conspicuous place in the premises of the LGU pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 01-
2020 dated April 6, 2020.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to coordinate with the
LDRRM Officer and City Engineer to immediately process the payment of
obligations and liquidations under the LDRRMF - STF and strictly adhere to the
provisions of Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that, henceforth, the City Accountant, check the completeness of documents in processing fund transfers to LGUs in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 2016-002
observations.managementAction: A MOA was already required for fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City General Services Officer instruct all heads of offices to require their drivers to accomplish properly the prescribed form for the Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption to ensure that all the data needed to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed for the period are provided. We further recommended that she require them to submit the reports to the Auditor monthly, supported with the Driver’s Trip Tickets.
observations.managementAction: Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption were already submitted to the Audit Team monthly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) comply with the documentary requirements for all disbursements in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; (b) prepare a well-planned procurement program to ensure adherence to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR; (c) refrain from incurring expenses without appropriations; and (d) exercise due prudence in the use of the government’s scarce resources in order to avoid unnecessary expenditures and henceforth, adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Personnel in charge of processing payments for the athletic activities were instructed to observe applicable regulations in future transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (i) conduct regular monitoring and assessment of the targets and strategies as stated in the SWMP to ensure the efficient management of solid wastes; (ii) enforce strictly the City Ordinances and strategies to ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; and (iii) involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of the 10-Year SWMP in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted massive IEC campaign through recorida, house to house distribution of flyers, leaflets, handouts etc., in partnership with the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require (a) the CGSO to conduct a physical count of supplies and materials semestrally and to prepare the RPCI; (b) all offices to report the issuances of supplies and materials as they take place using the RIS; (c) the CGSO to consolidate the RIS using the SSMI and submit the same to CAO as basis for recording supplies and materials issued; (d) the CAO and CGSO to maintain SLCs and stock cards, respectively, and to reconcile their records regularly; and (e) the CAO to make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the inventory accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The offices were already using the RIS to report the issuances of supplies and materials. The consolidation of the RIS in the SSMI was ongoing.
observations.reason: The delay was due to the lack of manpower in view of the retirement of many GSO personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CEO provide the CAO with copies of the report on completed projects at least a quarterly basis, together with the certificates of completion.
