Likewise, we recommended that the CAO assign personnel to focus on the verification of the items for identification in order to effect the necessary adjustments and meantime, disclose the same in the Notes to the Financial Statements. We further recommended that the CAO verify the CIP accounts against available records and henceforth, maintain complete subsidiary ledgers to support the balance of the accounts appearing in the general ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The CEO had partially provided copies of certificates of completion. The CAO personnel is in the process of reconciling the items for identification based on the available records.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records for prior years' balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant regularly monitor and demand submission of liquidation of funds transferred to other LGUs, and thereafter, draw a journal voucher to take up the expenses in order to present fairly the Due from LGUs and the related expense accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring of the liquidation of funds was conducted by the Accounting Office and the balances was in progress.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records for the prior years’ balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management assign or designate relevant officials who will be responsible for the conduct of a feasibility study and a five-year BP for the operation of the septage facility in accordance with the procedures provided in Items 4.2 and 4.6 of the Manual.
observations.managementAction: A feasibility study was already prepared.
observations.reason: Time constraints and lack of personnel to handle the job as the response to COVID-19 pandemic was prioritized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide additional personnel in the Office of the BAC to assist in the preparation of reports, especially the PMR to be approved by the LCE and submitted to the GPPB within the prescribed period. We further recommended that the BAC Secretariat post all the NOAs and NTPs in the PhilGEPS pursuant to Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The GSO already requested for additional personnel to work on the posting backlogs including the preparation of PMRs.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower because there are only two members of the BAC Secretariat who also perform other functions in addition to their functions as BAC Secretariat.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Council through the LDRRM Officer IV in coordination with CSWDO, CHO, CPDO, CEO and other concerned offices comply with the standards provided in the Disaster Preparedness Minimum Standards, Volume 2.
observations.managementAction: The construction of the ₱35M Evacuation Center and Multi-Purpose Building, designed as a disaster-resilient facility, in Barangay Batinguel was ongoing. Procurement of required equipage and implementation of other projects were ongoing.
observations.reason: The COVID-19 pandemic and lack of manpower in the CDRRMO caused the suspension of the implementation of some LDRRM projects, programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide the three staff of the LDRRMO with qualified personnel in accordance with the position titles, qualification standards and salary grades set under CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: Hiring process of personnel was ongoing.
observations.reason: The hiring and promotions of qualified personnel for the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office were temporarily on hold during the year due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS thoroughly review the GPB, paying particular attention on the output performance targets of PPAs that should be doable within the year in accordance with Section 8.2 of the PCW- NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
observations.managementAction: Only 52% of the PPAs were implemented during the year. Implementation of some PPAs, like capability building seminars and trainings were temporarily suspended due to COVID-19 restrictions.
observations.reason: Implementation of some PPAs were temporarily suspended due to COVID-19 restrictions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and effect adjusting entries to reclassify the erroneous accounts to their proper asset accounts and to take up Prior Period Adjustment accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office was still in the process of retrieving supporting documents as bases in taking up the adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of documents to support adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to avail of the remedies provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160 to enforce collection of delinquent taxes and fill up vacancies, especially for core positions at the CTO to augment its manpower requirement. The CTO had also issued warrants of levy.
observations.managementAction: The CTO continually send RPT billings but huge amount of RPT delinquencies remained uncollected. The CTO had also recommended the filling up of vacant positions at the CTO. Hiring of personnel to fill up the vacant positions was likewise suspended due to COVID-19 emergency.
observations.reason: Necessary steps leading to auction sale was suspended due to DOF Department Circular suspending implementation; hiring of personnel was likewise suspended due to COVID-19 emergency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management deduct from salaries and other benefits accruing to its employees authorized and other deductions which in no case shall reduce their monthly net take home pay to an amount prescribed under the existing law.
observations.managementAction: The HR Office had been steadily coherent in such implementation thus, the percentage of employees/ borrowers with below the 5K NTHP threshold had gradually reduced, particularly for the permanent and casual employees.
observations.reason: Some employees were already bound to the terms and conditions of the financial institutions from where they borrowed funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management designate a responsible employee to prepare a Five-Year BP and FSSE report for each LEE in accordance with the LEE Manual as basis for the LCE and Sangguniang Panlungsod in coming up with a decision on whether to continue the LEE operations or other related actions to make them profitable to ensure the efficient and effective provision of basic services and facilities to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: A multi-tasking system was adopted by the City Economic Enterprise Office to handle the preparation of FSSE Report. The Office of the Human Resource Department and the City Mayor requested for qualified personnel.
observations.reason: The request for qualified personnel was awaiting decision on whether to continue the LEE operations; procurement of required personnel was awaiting the decision of the City Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the CPDO and City Engineer to fast track the implementation of projects under the 20% DF by addressing the causes of delay, like insufficient funding requirements while requiring the barangays to implement their projects. We further recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, properly plan the projects for inclusion in the AIP and budget allocation, and closely monitor project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Of the 28 projects funded under the 2018/ 20% DF for CY 2020, 11 were fully implemented, four were on-going and eight were not yet implemented. Civil Engineers were assigned to prepare the program of works for the barangay-funded projects. Moreover, meetings with the barangays were conducted by the CEO to assist them in addressing the causes of delays in the project implementation.
observations.reason: Seven projects lacked funding requirements while drainage canal projects could not be implemented due to problems in right-of-way.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CEO cause the timely submission of the COCs and reports on the disbursement for fuel and lubricants component of the various projects in order to fully liquidate the unliquidated funds and the unspent portion amounting to ₱55,138.43 be returned to the Source Agency.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation of the fuel and lubricants components of the projects was ongoing. Out of the ₱13.5M unliquidated funds, ₱614,161.43 was already returned to the Source Agency.
observations.reason: Fuel, oil and lubricants consumption reports were not regularly submitted to the City Accounting Office for recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the following courses of action to establish the accuracy/validity of the PPE accounts in the ensuing year: Direct the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the physical inventory of properties and prepare the RPCPPE; Direct the City Accountant and City General Services Officer to reconcile their records with the RPCPPE and effect the necessary adjustments in either the property records or in the books of accounts; and Require the Accounting Office to identify items marked as “for identification” and to update the PPELCs as this subsidiary record shall support the PPE controlling account balance.
observations.managementAction: Prior to year 2008, the Accounting Office has huge amount of property items for identification without specific details and description of equipment and its costs. The absence of list of equipment and properties per classification from the Accounting Office posed difficulty in the reconciliation. However, the GSO Inventory Team conducted an inventory and submitted RPCPPE during the year.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records for the previous years hamper the reconciliation process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant: Review all outstanding payables, validate and ascertain supporting documents, and revert those which are not supported with valid claims and those which are aged two years or more; Investigate accounts payable which have remained dormant and those with negative balances and determine the cause/s and prepare adjusting journal entries for all errors noted; and Henceforth, record accounts payable in accordance with the regulations provided in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and maintain subsidiary ledgers indicating the name of creditor including the address to allow confirmation.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office reviewed and validated the Accounts Payable balances and adjustments were made to Accounts Payable. Verification of financial statements and its supporting schedules showed that payable account still included dormant accounts as well as negative balances. Some items are still for reconciliation with the available records.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records for the previous years hamper the reconciliation process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the immediate liquidation of cash advances by sending out another demand letters signed both by the City Accountant and by the City Mayor or his authorized representative; The Accounting Office verify and analyze the negative balances noted in the Due from Officers and Employees. If there was over-settlement of cash advances or error in recording, effect the necessary adjustments; and The City Accountant request write-off for long outstanding cash advances, especially of those who are deceased pursuant of the guidelines/procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office verified and reconciled cash advance accounts. The CAO submitted demand letters to concerned accountable officers.
observations.reason: Some required documents for the request for write-off, like death certificates of accountable officers were already secured by the Accounting Office. Some accounts had to be reconciled especially those pertaining to the previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant review and analyze the list of outstanding checks and regularly draw a JEV for the cancellation of stale checks to reflect the correct monthly balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments amounting to ₱5,038.00 were partially taken up and suppliers/creditors were already informed about the stale checks.
observations.reason: Some checks were still in the hands of suppliers/creditors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant (a) verify the reconciling items and draw a JEV to adjust all valid reconciling items pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; (b) conduct a thorough verification of the dormant bank accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments; and (c) provide copies of the bank reconciliation statements to the depository banks so that any discrepancies noted between the records of the banks and of the City can be immediately resolved.
observations.managementAction: Various reconciling items with the DBP amounting to ₱392,470.60 were already adjusted in September 2018. Additional reconciling items were cleared in the amount of ₱392,470.60 both cash in bank and in books.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving the supporting documents, especially those pertaining to prior years’ transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management expedite the implementation of all projects, programs and activities (PPAs) under the LDRRMF to strengthen the capacity of the City Government to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects of disasters or calamity.
observations.managementAction: The Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer assigned a staff to monitor all activities, projects and programs under the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: The implementation of LDRRM PPAs were sometimes not given priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003; In the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame; Enforce strictly the City’s projects and programs relative to solid waste management and ensure 100% compliance thereof; and Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer’s Office undertook open dumpsite closure activities, such as site grading, stabilization and final cover of dumpsite; construction of perimeter fence, lighting, fabrication and installation of signages and the construction of the guard house at the dumpsite area was completed and the Special Enforcement Unit and the Environment and Natural Resources Office (ENRO) enforcers are on duty 24 hours daily to prevent dumpsite scavenging while the rehabilitation of the open dumpsite is ongoing. The new Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) located at Barangay Candau-ay was completed while the establishment of a sanitary landfill in the same site was still awaiting for the issuance of the Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC). The City entered into a Memorandum of Agreement with LGU-Bayawan City for the dumping of the City’s residual wastes in the latter’s sanitary landfill. The CENRO conducted massive IEC activities and additional 100 scavengers were employed by the City to work at the MRF sorting area to stop them from scavenging.
observations.reason: The COVID-19 pandemic and lack of manpower in the CDRRMO caused the suspension of the implementation of some LDRRM projects, programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management order the SDO personnel to desist from receiving payments for loan amortization from people’s organizations and to instruct all borrowers to directly pay to the CTO. We also recommended that the City Treasurer prepare a separate report of collections together with duplicate copies of the official receipt to the Accounting Office for posting in the individual subsidiary ledgers of the borrowers. We further recommended that the City Accountant immediately send demand letters to debtors to ascertain collectability of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Sectoral Desk Office had stopped receiving loan payments from People’s Organization (POs). The CAO in coordination with CTO sent demand and collection letters to various POs. Four organizations had fully paid their accounts.
observations.reason: The accounts of other organizations remained dormant because most of them were already dissolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the DSWD to observe existing policy on program monitoring activities to immediately address any problem, ensure loan amortization payment in accordance with the loan payment schedule and rollback of the seed capital assistance. We also recommended that the City Accountant send demand letters to SKAs to settle their monthly/quarterly/semi-annually payments.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent to various organizations/individuals concerned. On the other hand, some borrowers provided proofs of payments for deposits made directly thru the banks.
observations.reason: The addresses of the members of dissolved organizations are unknown, hence the delay in sending out demand letters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management order the conduct of complete physical count of PPEs as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21 dated February 17, 2016 and other existing regulations. Results of the physical inventory should be reconciled with the accounting and property records to present accurate balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office conducted an inventory of all real and movable properties as of June 30, 2016. CAO and GSO records are still for reconciliation due to incomplete records and supporting documents.
observations.reason: Both records of the CAO and GSO are still for reconciliation due to incomplete records and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit travel orders for his travels listed in Annex A to the audit team and henceforth, secure permission from the Provincial Governor every time he travels outside the Province of Negros Oriental pursuant to Section 96(b) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Requested travel orders from the Provincial Governor to support his travels outside the province.
observations.reason: The request had not been acted upon yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: Require the City Legal Officer in coordination with the City General Services Officer to initiate the titling of the real properties to avoid possible ownership disputes in the future and to enable the Accounting Office to record their cost in the books of accounts; Instruct the City Accountant to ensure that all recorded transactions have been adequately supported by valid documents and to maintain subsidiary ledgers to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and Require the General Services Office to maintain complete records and documents of all the properties of the City Government pursuant to Section 490 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The CLO went over all files of lot acquisitions from the 30 barangays, and identified which among these had already been issued Certificates Authorizing Registration (CARs) by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR). To date, more than 90 CARs already exist. The CLO will look into these in greater detail. Once the e-CARs are procured, the CLO can then continue the individual titling of these lots in favor of the City of Dumaguete. After which, the CLO would provide copies of the TCTs to the City Accounting and the General Services Office for proper recording of their cost in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Recent BIR requirements/developments in processing of lot titles had to be complied with; retrieving of supporting documents took time and required compliance with BIR electronic CAR requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant, Budget Officer, City Treasurer and all other officials responsible for the charges against the 5% LDRRMF to refund the amount totaling ₱1,125,112.85 and henceforth, adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 in the use of the 5% LDRRMF to avoid audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant’s Office sought the assistance of the CLO on this regard. The CAO and CTO recommended to Management to formally initiate the restitution of the amount of ₱1,125,112.85 from the General Fund to LDRRMF Fund in order to replenish or refund the 5% LDRRMF for the subject purchases. It was proposed that the City Council pass a special appropriation ordinance for this matter.
observations.reason: Awaiting Management’s approval on the restitution of the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fully and immediately comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to promote transparency and accountability, to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern, and to avoid the penalty clause therein and always review the report and check the signboards before putting them up to ensure the correctness of all information thereon.
observations.managementAction: Project signages were already put up on various projects of the City.
observations.reason: Some lacking information on the project signages like the dates the projects started were not taken into consideration yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately undertake the recommendations of the Technical Working Group to make the necessary adjustments of the room volume to provide adequate insulation. Likewise, undertake all necessary measures, if still possible to make the storage facility operational/functional. Otherwise, determine the official/staff responsible/liable and impose appropriate administrative sanction.
observations.managementAction: A job order on supply, re-insulation, and installation of 800mm x 3750mm x 3000H (external dimensions) of walk-in cold-room storage facility with an approved budget totaling ₱720,000.00 was already prepared and underwent the required procurement process. As of February 5, 2020, the job order was returned to the CEO because the negotiated price of supplier exceeded the Approved Budget for Contract (ABC).
observations.reason: The Cost of the JO as per supplier’s quotation exceeded the budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to give priority to and intensify the review and analysis of the existing reconciling items and adjust those which are clearly identified, based on the existing bank statements, including the set-up of accountability for the double issuance of check replacement.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of various bank accounts had been prioritized. In December 2016, bank reconciling items amounting to ₱392,470.60 were already adjusted. Additional reconciling items were cleared in the amount of ₱392,470.60.
observations.reason: Reconciliation was difficult due to unavailability of documents and records, particularly those items pertaining to prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to give priority to and intensify the review and analysis of the existing reconciling items and adjust those which are clearly identified, based on the existing bank statements, including the set-up of accountability for the double issuance of check replacement.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office had been working on these reconciling items. Ongoing partial adjustments had been made and requests for copies of bank statements were made to the banks.
observations.reason: Lacking documents necessary to facilitate identification of unrecorded deposits and disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant analyze and validate the dormant accounts and accounts for “ID” classified under Due from NGOs/POs and the unsupported balances reflected in the subsidiary ledgers. If the transactions analysis/review of the accounts is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances from the Commission on Audit, in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: Identification of dormant accounts was ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents particularly pertaining to the previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: Immediately submit the audited Statement of Liquidation to the various source agencies for proper reporting and accounting of the funds received and utilized and to return any unutilized amount pursuant to the terms and conditions of the MOA and COA Circular No. 94-013; and Monitor regularly the status/liquidation of fund transfers received from NGAs based on duly documented transactions and to keep a separate and complete file of documents/reports for each fund received.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing. The CAO submitted quarterly reports and coordinated with the CEO to fast track the liquidation of transferred funds.
observations.reason: Lack of certificates of completion for the completed projects and clearance from the City Mayor to return the balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: Conduct review and reconciliation of all Due to Other NGAs accounts by ascertaining the status of project/activities completion, balances and status of liquidation; and Require the City Accountant to likewise verify and investigate the status of the funds by confirming the balances with the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Update, review and reconciliation of balances in the subsidiary ledgers was ongoing. The status and liquidation of projects and activities with no movement for quite some time were verified.
observations.reason: The subsidiary ledgers of this account were not updated due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately release the RPT shares due to the barangays. Henceforth, adherence to Section 272 (d) of the Local Government Code is encouraged and require the City Accountant to determine the actual status of the 50% share of the community taxes and if found to be erroneously recorded, cause the transfer of the amount to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to be used for other activities/programs.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office had been monitoring the funds released to the barangays to ensure timely and immediate transfer of the share of real property taxes to beneficiary-barangays. Reconciliation was ongoing to determine the correctness of the account.
observations.reason: Difficulty in gathering supporting documents to determine the correctness of the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to determine the composition of the amount set up as Items In Transit converted from the in-process accounts in 2002 as well as the payments or liquidations thereof, and to adjust the Items In Transit account in order to fairly present the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing reconciliation to determine the composition of the amount set up in 2002. The CAO was working back from prior years’ records. Incomplete documents caused the delay in the reconciliation.
observations.reason: Unavailability of required documents in the prior years hampered the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to request from the GSO for a list of assets acquired from 2002 to 2007, to compute the depreciation expense for all depreciable assets for the current audit period as well as from 2002 up to 2006 using the Prior Years’ Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation accounts in order to fairly present the asset and equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation expenses were already recognized for fixed assets acquired from 2008 up to the present. Identification of depreciable assets purchased from CY 2002 to 2007 was ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of available documents to support adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider revising the rental rates of stall spaces in the public market to make it a viable enterprise and in the process, take into consideration the volume of trade or business in each section in order to come up with more equitable and just rates, and avoid charging to market operations the salaries and other emoluments of personnel who are not actually rendering services therein.
observations.managementAction: The market management implemented in January 2011, an ordinance passed by the City Council on July 4, 2002 imposing a 25% adjustment on all new stall awardees and five percent increase every three years thereafter. The rate increase applies only to the stall awardees after the enactment of said ordinance.
observations.reason: Increase of rental rates had not been imposed to the old stall awardees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant be required to identify the composition of the portion of government equity representing the costs of the completed projects and fixed assets and transfer the same to the General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The assigned personnel in the City Accountant’s Office had been working on the liquidation of completed projects under Trust Fund with the offices implementing the projects.
observations.reason: Retrieving of supporting documents took time and some required records were unavailable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the City Accountant to effect the appropriate adjustments to reconcile accounts Due to Other Funds and Due From Other Funds.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts was ongoing. The CAO had to work back from the prior years’ records. Incomplete documents caused the delay in the reconciliation.
observations.reason: Reconciliation took some time because the CAO had to work back from the prior years’ records. Incomplete documents caused the delay in the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management deduct from the salaries and other benefits accruing to its employees authorized other deductions which in no case shall reduce their monthly net take home pay to an amount prescribed under the existing law.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management had yet to determine authorized and how much to deduct from the salaries of employees.

observations.recommendation: For the PDAF utilized for intelligence purposes, we recommended immediate refund of the amount of ₱500,000.00 so that these could be used instead for bona fide pro-poor projects and programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: Discussion was done by top Management that the amount of ₱500,000.00 will be restituted from the General Fund-Unappropriated Surplus. The Notice of Disallowance (ND) is on appeal. Settlement had been done in July 2012; the liquidation documents were sent to COA Central Office for audit and action and Credit Notice, and Management action on the restitution of the amount.
observations.reason: Awaiting the COA Central Office audit and Credit Notice; Management’s action on the restitution of the amount pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require (a) the CGSO to
conduct a physical count of supplies and materials semestrally and to prepare the
RPCI; (b) all offices to report the issuances of supplies and materials as they take
place using the RIS; (c) the CGSO to consolidate the RIS using the SSMI and
submit the same to CAO as basis for recording supplies and materials issued; (d)
the CAO and CGSO to maintain SLCs and stock cards, respectively, and to
reconcile their records regularly; and (e) the CAO to make the necessary
adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the inventory accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommended and the CEO agreed to provide the CAO with copies of the
report on completed projects on at least a quarterly basis, together with the
certificates of completion. Likewise, we recommended that the CAO assign
personnel to focus on the verification of the items for identification in order to effect
the necessary adjustments and meantime, disclose the same in the Notes to the
Financial Statements. We further recommended and the CAO agreed to verify the
CIP accounts against available records and henceforth, maintain complete
subsidiary ledgers to support the balance of the accounts appearing in the general
ledgers.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to regularly monitor and
demand submission of liquidation of funds transferred to other LGUs, and
thereafter, draw a journal voucher to take up the expenses in order to present fairly
the Due from LGUs and the related expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed that, henceforth, check the
completeness of documents in processing fund transfers to LGUs in accordance with
Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 2016-002 dated June 14, 2012 and
May 31, 2016, respectively, to ensure the regularity of the releases of funds to other
LGUs.

We recommended and Management agreed to assign or designate relevant
officials who will be responsible for the conduct of a feasibility study and a five-year
BP for the operation of the septage facility in accordance with the procedures
provided in Items 4.2 and 4.6 of the Manual.

We recommended and Management agreed to provide additional personnel in
the Office of the BAC to assist in the preparation of reports, especially the PMR to
be approved by the LCE and submitted to the GPPB within 14 days after the end of
each semester. We further recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to post
all the NOAs and NTPs in the PhilGEPS pursuant to Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of
the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the City General Services Officer agreed to instruct all
heads of offices to require their drivers to accomplish properly the prescribed form
for the Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel
Consumption to ensure that all the data needed to determine the reasonableness of
fuel consumed for the period are provided. We further recommended and she
agreed to require them to submit the reports to the Auditor monthly, supported
with the Driver’s Trip Tickets.

We recommended and the LDRRM Council through the LDRRM Officer IV
in coordination with CSWDO, CHO, CPDO, CEO and other concerned offices
agreed to comply with the standards provided in the Disaster Preparedness
Minimum Standards, Volume 2.

We recommended and the City agreed to hire qualified personnel to occupy
the position of the three staff of the LDRRMO in accordance with the position titles,
qualification standards and salary grades set under Section 6.0 of NDRRMC-DILG-
DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to thoroughly review the GPB,
paying particular attention on the output performance targets of PPAs that should
be doable within the year in accordance with Section 8.2 of the PCW-DEDA and
DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommended that Management secure authority from the SP for the time deposit accounts in the General Fund and terminate the existing accounts in the Trust fund pursuant to Section 21 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The time deposits under Trust Fund were already terminated and transferred to a current account through an SP authorization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and effect adjusting entries to reclassify the erroneous accounts to their proper asset accounts and to take up Prior Period Adjustment for the expenses incurred in CY 2016 in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs per PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office was in the process of retrieving supporting documents as bases in taking up the adjustments.
observations.reason: Lack of documents to effect adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CLO and CGSO henceforth, furnish immediately the CAO with the Deeds of Donation, Appraisal Committee Valuation Report and other supporting documents as bases in recording donated properties in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The CLO had furnished CAO with the supporting documents and the latter recorded in the books the fair market value of ₱20,815,040.20 based on the City Assessor’s Office Tax Declaration per Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2019-12-0350.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer immediately release the 30% share from the basic RPT to the 30 barangays pursuant to Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160. We further recommended and the BAO personnel agreed to prepare immediately the Summary of RPTs and related supporting documents for the release of the 30% RPT share to the barangays.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurers Office (CTO) sent demand letters to delinquent taxpayers in coordination with the City Legal Office for appropriate action. Moreover, the City Treasurer recommended the filling up of vacant positions at the CTO.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to do the tasks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer immediately release the 30% share from the basic RPT to the 30 barangays pursuant to Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160. We further recommended and the BAO personnel agreed to prepare immediately the Summary of RPTs and related supporting documents for the release of the 30% RPT share to the barangays.
observations.managementAction: The CTO and CAO already implemented the immediate release of the RPT shares to the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management increase the fidelity bonds of the DOs to an amount sufficient to cover their maximum accountabilities, in accordance with the revised schedule of bond premium rates provided by the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances granted to the DOs were already within the maximum cash accountabilities and fidelity bonds of DOs were already based on the rates prescribed by the Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management designate a responsible employee to prepare a Five-Year BP and FSSE report for each LEE in accordance with the LEE Manual as basis for the LCE and Sangguniang Panlungsod in coming up with a decision on whether to continue the LEE operations or other related actions to make them financially self-sufficient and eventually profitable to ensure the efficient and effective provision of basic services and facilities to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: A multi-tasking system was adopted by the CEED to handle the preparation of the FSSE Report. Likewise, the City Economic Enterprise Officer requested for qualified personnel to prepare the Human Resource Department and required BP for the LEEs. Department and the Mayor.
observations.reason: The Office requested qualified personnel to do the specific jobs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the CPDO and City Engineer to fast track the implementation of projects under the 20% DF by addressing the causes of delay, like insufficient funds, issues on road right-of-way, lack of POW, and requiring the barangays to implement their projects. We further recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, properly plan the projects for inclusion in the AIP and budget allocation, and closely monitor project implementation.
observations.managementAction: Of the 28 projects funded under the 20% DF for CY 2017 2018, 11 were fully implemented, four were on-going and eight were not yet implemented. Some barangays lacked funding requirements while the Barangays had started implementing their own infrastructure projects previously undertaken by the City Engineer’s Office (CEO), thus easing up the workload of the CEO. Civil Engineers were assigned to prepare the program of works for the barangay-funded projects. Moreover, meetings with the barangays were conducted by the CEO to assist them in addressing the causes of delays in the project implementation.
observations.reason: Seven projects lacked funding requirements while the proposals took time and some lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Accountant, henceforth, the unexpended prior year’s balance of the LDRRMF for QRF and MOOE be transferred from the General Fund to the Trust Fund at year-end pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended prior year’s balances of the LDRRMF for QRF and MOOE were transferred to the Special Trust Fund in February 2019 through the issuance of Check No. 63610830.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) comply with the documentary requirements for all disbursements in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; (b) prepare a well-planned procurement program to ensure adherence to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR; (c) refrain from incurring expenses without appropriations; and (d) exercise due prudence in the use of the government’s scarce resources in order to avoid unnecessary expenditures and henceforth, adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The lacking documentary requirements related to the CVIRAA were already submitted. Discussion and corrective steps were undertaken but full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CEO cause the timely submission of the COCs and reports on the disbursement for fuel and lubricants component of the various projects in order to fully liquidate the funds received and/or return to the SA the unspent transferred funds for completed projects.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation of the fuel and lubricants components of the projects was ongoing. Out of the ₱13.5M unliquidated funds, ₱614,161.43 was fully liquidated and the unspent portion amounting to ₱55,138.43 was already returned to the Source Agency. The CAO prepared necessary reports.
observations.reason: Fuel, oil and lubricants reports were not regularly submitted; some liquidations and returns were ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the following courses of action from time to time reconciled their records with the RPCPPE: • Direct the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the physical inventory of properties and prepare the RPCPPE; • Direct the City Accountant and City General Services Officer to reconcile their records with the RPCPPE and effect the necessary adjustments in either the property records or in the books of accounts; and • Require the Accounting Office to identify items marked as “for identification” and to update the PPELCs as this subsidiary record shall support the PPE controlling account balance.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted a report on the physical count of PPE. The City Accounting Office reviewed and validated the PPE accounts and corrected their PPE records based on the actual physical count, and sought authority from the City Council to write off unidentified PPE balances.
observations.reason: Lack of complete documents for accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant: (a) Review all outstanding payables, validate available supporting documents, and revert those which are not supported with valid claims and those which are aged over two years as per JEV No. 2018-07-0213; (b) Investigate accounts payable which have remained dormant and those with negative balances and determine the cause/s and prepare adjusting journal entries for all errors noted; and (c) Henceforth, record accounts payable in accordance with the regulations provided in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, and maintain subsidiary ledgers indicating the name of creditor including the address to allow confirmation.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office reviewed and validated the Accounts Payable and adjusted accounts payable over two years as per JEV No. 2018-07-0213. As of December 31, 2019, the balance of Accounts Payable amounted to ₱101,626,417.67.
observations.reason: Verification of the financial statements and its adjustments were made to accounts payable and negative balances. Some items are still for reconciliation with available records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: • The Accounting Office verify and analyze the negative balances noted in the Due from Officers and Employees. If there was over-settlement of cash advances or error in recording, effect the necessary adjustments; and • The City Accountant request write-off for long outstanding cash advances, especially of those who are deceased or outside of government service, pursuant of the guidelines/procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19 2016.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent out to concerned officials and employees. Likewise, the CAO coordinated with the CLO as to exploring appropriate actions to collect refunds/settlements of cash advances especially those outside of government service. The CAO recommended write off long outstanding cash advances and the Accounting Office was working on identification and possible write-off. Gathering of supporting documents is still ongoing.
observations.reason: Gathering of supporting documents is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant review and analyze the list of outstanding checks and regularly draw a JEV for the cancellation of stale checks to reflect the correct monthly balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments amounting to ₱5,038.00 were partially taken up. Further, suppliers/creditors were informed about the stale checks.
observations.reason: Some checks were still in the hands of suppliers/creditors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant (a) verify the reconciling items and draw a JEV to adjust all valid reconciling items pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; (b) conduct a thorough verification of the dormant bank accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments; and (c) provide copies of the bank reconciliation statements to the depository banks so that any discrepancies noted between the records of the banks and of the City can be immediately resolved.
observations.managementAction: Various reconciling items with the DBP amounting to ₱392,470.60 were already adjusted in September 2018.
observations.reason: Retrieval of supporting documents, especially for prior years' transactions, was difficult.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management expedite the implementation of all projects, programs and activities (PPAs) under the LDRRMF to strengthen the capacity of the City Government to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects of disasters or calamity.
observations.managementAction: The Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) assigned a staff to monitor all activities, projects and programs under the LDRRMF. Implementation was ongoing; some activities were implemented but manpower constraints affected full implementation.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower in the Office of the LDRRMO, implementation of LDRRM PPAs of LDDRM were sometimes not given priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: • Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003. Meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame; • Enforce strictly the City’s programs relative to solid waste management and ensure 100% compliance thereof; and • Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Safe Closure & Rehabilitation Plan of the dumpsite was ongoing. The operation of the dumpsite was on the northern side only while the closure activities were going on in the western and southern sides. The ENRO conducted Information, Education & Communication projects and activities, “operation dakop”, inspection activities in all the barangays and issued citation tickets to violators. The City purchased a new lot at Barangay Candau-ay for the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and sanitary landfill and is waiting for the Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC).
observations.reason: Some barangays did not have the required materials to help facilitate proper waste segregation management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management order the SDO personnel to desist from receiving payments for loan amortization from people’s organizations and to instruct all borrowers to directly pay to the CTO. We also recommended that the City Treasurer prepare a separate report of collections together with duplicate copies of the official receipt to the Accounting Office for posting in the individual subsidiary ledgers of the borrowers. Further, the City Accountant be required to immediately send demand letters to debtors to ascertain collectability of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The CAO in coordination with the CTO sent demand and collection letters to various POs. Four organizations had fully paid their accounts. Some borrowers provided proofs of payments through depositing directly to the bank. The City Accountant prepared reports and coordinated for posting.
observations.reason: The accounts of other organizations/creditors remained dormant because most of them were already dissolved. Difficulty in locating the members due to dissolution of associations caused delay in sending out demand letters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: require the DSWD to observe existing policy on program monitoring activities to immediately address any problem, ensure loan amortization payment in accordance with the loan payment schedule and the Self-Employment Assistance-Kaunlaran (SEA-K) Program; and request the City Accountant to send demand letters to SKAs to settle their monthly/quarterly/semi-annually rollback of the seed capital assistance.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent to concerned associations/individuals. On the other hand, some borrowers provided proofs of payments through depositing directly to the bank.
observations.reason: The difficulty in gathering the supporting documents to determine the members due to dissolution of associations also caused the delay in sending demands.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the conduct of complete physical count of PPEs as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21 dated February 17, 2016 and other existing regulations. Results of the physical inventory should be reconciled with the accounting and property records to present accurate balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office conducted an inventory of all real and movable properties as of June 30, 2016. There was an effort to write-off balances of PPE based on the actual physical count by seeking authority from the City Council but still pending action.
observations.reason: Incomplete supporting documents. The request for write-off pending action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) prioritize the finding of a suitable location and allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill; (b) enforce waste segregation at source; and (c) implement the recommendation contained under Finding No. 15 by assigning enforcers to stop dumpsite scavenging, and providing other means of livelihood to scavengers taking into consideration their occupational skills.
observations.managementAction: The City purchased a new lot at Barangay Candau-ay for the MRF and sanitary landfill. The ENRO strictly enforced waste segregation at source, conducted IEC campaigns and deployed enforcers and requested assistance from City Police Force, Special Enforcement Unit, Task Force and Sectoral desk to stop dumpsite scavenging. Some scavengers were employed by the City to work at the composting area to stop them from scavenging.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit travel orders for his travels listed in Annex A to the audit team and henceforth, secure permission from the Provincial Governor every time he travels outside the Province of Negros Oriental pursuant to Section 96(b) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Requested travel orders from the Provincial Governor to support his travels outside the province.
observations.reason: The request had not been acted upon yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) conduct regular monitoring and assessment of the targets and strategies as stated in the SWMP to ensure the efficient management of solid wastes; (b) enforce strictly the City Ordinances and strategies to ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; and (c) involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of the 10-Year SWMP in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Safe Closure & Rehabilitation Plan of the Candau-ay open dumpsite was ongoing. The CSWMB approved the 2019-2029 Ten-Year SWMP in July 2019 and it was endorsed to NSWMC. The CENRO conducted IEC campaigns and enforcers were deployed to apprehend violators and ensure segregation at source and proper waste disposal and management.
observations.reason: The 2019-2029 Ten-Year Ecological SWMP was not approved by NSWMC because it was not in accordance with the prescribed format by DENR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) require the City Legal Officer in coordination with the City General Services Officer to initiate the titling of the real properties to avoid possible ownership disputes in the future and to enable the accounting office to record their cost in the books of accounts; (b) instruct the City Accountant to ensure that all recorded transactions have been adequately supported by valid documents and to maintain subsidiary ledgers to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and (c) require the General Services Office to maintain complete records and documents of all the properties of the City Government pursuant to Section 490 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The City Legal Office worked on the titling of all lots acquired by the City, several of which were acquired decades back. The GSO kept all the titles while all deeds of conveyances were forwarded by the CLO to the CAO for proper recording. Subsidiary Ledgers were maintained as far as 2008 transactions. The CLO retrieved files of lot acquisitions and identified those which had already been issued Certificates Authorizing Registration (CARs) by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR). More than 90 transactions already have CAR while a lot needed update since the BIR now requires the electronic CAR or e-CAR.
observations.reason: Securing the supporting documents for prior years' land acquisitions and donations took time and resources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to refund the amount of ₱1,125,112.85 and henceforth, adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 in the use of the 5% LDRRMF to avoid audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management suspended all disbursements charged against PDAF and already returned portions of the unexpended balances to the Bureau of Treasury pending certification of completion from Implementing Agency and certification of undelivered or cancelled items in the case of unimplemented projects. The CAO and CTO recommended Management to formally initiate the restitution of the amount of ₱1,125,112.85 from General Fund to LDRRMF Fund in order to replenish or refund the 5% LDRRMF for the subject purchases. It was proposed that the City Council pass a special appropriation ordinance for this matter.
observations.reason: Awaiting management’s action on the restitution of the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City desist from disbursing the unexpended PDAF balance of ₱1,941,164.20 and immediately return the total amount to the Bureau of Treasury pending submission of certification of completion from Implementing Agency for proper verification and submission of certification of undelivered or cancelled items for unimplemented projects.
observations.managementAction: Management suspended all disbursements charged against PDAF and already returned portions of the unexpended balances to the Bureau of Treasury. The City Accounting Office reconciled and prepared the reports for the 15 PDAF projects and certification of undelivered or cancelled items in the case of unimplemented projects.
observations.reason: Not all unexpended balances were returned to the Bureau of Treasury; certification of completion and supporting documents for some projects pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fully and immediately comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to promote transparency and accountability, to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern, and to avoid the penalty clause therein and always review the report and check the signboards before putting them up to ensure the correctness of all information thereon.
observations.managementAction: The City installed tarpaulin signages on various projects but required information like date project started and expected date of completion were not indicated. The City Accountants were reminded to review the reports and confirm signboards.
observations.reason: Some lacking information were not taken into consideration when posting the signages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately undertake the recommendations of the Technical Working Group to make the necessary adjustments of the room volume to provide adequate insulation. Likewise, undertake all necessary measures, if still possible to make the storage facility operational/functional. Otherwise, determine the official/staff responsible/liable and impose appropriate administrative sanction.
observations.managementAction: A job order on supply, insulation, and installation of 8000mm x 3750mm x 3000H (external dimensions) of walk-in cold storage was implemented. A job order was prepared but the supplier’s negotiated price was over the ABC. On February 5, 2020, the job order was returned to the CEO because the negotiated price of supplier was over the ABC.
observations.reason: The cost of the job order as per supplier’s quotation exceeded the approved budget; retrieval of supporting documents and finalization took time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant immediately prioritize the reconciliation and substantiation of the bank accounts which have not been confirmed by the depository banks.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of various bank accounts have been prioritized. In December 2016, reconciling items amounting to ₱392,470.60 were cleared in September 2018.
observations.reason: Unavailability of documents and supporting records hindered facilitation of reconciliation for prior years' transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office give priority to and intensify the review and analysis of the existing reconciling items and adjust those which are clearly identified, based on the existing bank statements, including the set-up of accountability for the double issuance of checks.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office made several requests for bank statements from the bank. Partial adjustments were made where possible. Various reconciling items were cleared.
observations.reason: Reconciliation was difficult due to unavailability of documents and supporting records; retrieval of prior year bank statements took time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant analyze and validate the dormant accounts and accounts for “ID” classified under Due from NGOs/POs and the unsupported balances reflected in the subsidiary ledgers. If the analysis/review of the accounts is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances from the Commission on Audit, in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: Identification of dormant accounts was ongoing. The City Accounting Office identified several transactions which occurred in the prior years and some supporting documents were retrieved. Adjustments were being processed as appropriate.
observations.reason: Lack of supporting documents; many transactions dated back to prior years; identification and validation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Immediately submit the audited Statement of Liquidation to the source agencies for proper reporting and accounting of the funds received and utilized and to return any unutilized amount pursuant to the terms and conditions of the MOA and COA Circular No. 94-013; and (b) Monitor regularly the status/liquidation of fund transfers received from NGAs based on duly documented transactions and to keep a separate and complete file of documents/reports for each fund received.
observations.managementAction: The CAO submitted quarterly reports and coordinated with the CEO to fast track the completion for liquidation of transferred funds. The CAO prepared liquidation reports and requested return of balances to the source agencies where applicable.
observations.reason: Update, review and reconciliation of balances in subsidiary ledgers ongoing; lack of manpower; some source agencies pending their certification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) Conduct review and reconciliation of all Due to Other NGAs accounts by project/activities completion, balances and status of liquidation; and (b) Require the City Accountant to likewise verify and investigate the status of the funds by confirming the balances with the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Update, review and reconciliation of subsidiary ledgers was ongoing. The CAO coordinated with source agencies for confirming balances and status of liquidation.
observations.reason: The subsidiary ledgers of this account were not updated due to lack of personnel; ongoing reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately release the RPT shares due to the barangays. Henceforth, adherence to Section 272 (d) of the Local Government Code is encouraged and require the City Accountant to determine the actual status of the 50% share of the community taxes and if found to be erroneously recorded, cause the transfer of the amount to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to be used for other activities/programs.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office had been monitoring the funds released to the barangays to ensure timely and immediate liquidation of transferred funds. Reconciliation was ongoing to determine the correctness of the account. The 50% Community Taxes amounting to ₱512,614.98 remained dormant from 2006 to present. Reconciliation was ongoing.
observations.reason: Difficulty in gathering supporting documents to determine correctness of the accounts. Reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to determine the composition of the amount set up as Items in Transit converted from the in-process accounts in 2002 as well as the payments or liquidations thereof, and to adjust the Items in Transit account in order to fairly present the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing reconciliation to determine the composition of the amount set up during in-transit converted from the in-process accounts in 2002 as well as the payments or liquidations thereof.
observations.reason: Unavailability of supporting documents for the payments or adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to request from the GSO for a list of assets acquired from 2002 to 2007, to compute the depreciation expense for all depreciable assets for the current audit period as well as from 2002 up to 2006 using the Prior Years’ Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation accounts in order to fairly present the asset and equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation expenses were already recognized for fixed assets acquired from 2008 to present. Identification of depreciable assets purchased from 2002 to 2007 and computation of accumulated depreciation for these assets was ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of available documents to support adjustments in the books of accounts; identification of prior year assets ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider revising the rental rates of stall spaces in the public market to make it a viable enterprise and in the process, take into consideration the volume of trade or business in each section in order to come up with more equitable and just rates, and avoid charging to market operations the salaries and other emoluments of personnel who are not actually rendering services therein.
observations.managementAction: The City implemented increase of rental rates in January 2011, an ordinance passed by the City Council on July 4, 2002 imposing a 25% adjustment on all new stall awardees and five percent increase every three years thereafter on the rental rate of the stall spaces at the Dumaguete City Public Market Complex. As embodied in the ordinance, the increase applies only to stall awardees after the enactment of said ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant be required to identify the composition of the amount set up as obligations which were converted in 2002 to the account Items in Transit under the New Government Accounting System, and to transfer the same to the General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The assigned personnel in the City Accountant’s Office had been working on the liquidation of completed projects and fixed assets and transfer the same to the General Fund books. Documents retrieval took time and projects were being reconciled.
observations.reason: The offices implementing the projects took time to complete the liquidation and provide supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Agriculturist be required to report monthly all births of animals owned by the City, to brand all animals purchased or produced, to require livestock technicians to be on call at all times to attend to sick animals, and to submit utilization reports for feeds and vitamins. Moreover, require the accountant to maintain livestock ledger cards and to update these monthly using the reports submitted by the City Agriculturist, and require the GSO to maintain property cards, and require Certificates of Ownership or Transfer for large cattle to be filed as reference for identifying each animal.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary Ledgers were maintained by the CAO and updated monthly. The CGSO designated an inventory team to maintain animals and breeding stocks property cards for animals acquired by the City. The City Agriculturist coordinated with the CGSO and City Agriculture Office with regards to ear-notching procedure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the City Accountant to effect the appropriate adjustments to reconcile accounts Due to Other Funds and Due From Other Funds.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts was ongoing. The CAO had to work back from prior years records to complete reconciliation. Prepared supporting documents were gathered.
observations.reason: Reconciliation took some time because the CAO had to work back from the prior years records. Incomplete documents caused the delay in the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to secure authority from the SP for the time
deposit accounts in the General Fund and terminate the existing accounts in the
Trust Fund pursuant to Section 21 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3,
1992.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to prepare and effect
adjusting entries to reclassify the erroneous accounts to their proper asset
accounts and to take up Prior Period Adjustment for the expenses incurred in
CY 2016 in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs per
PPSAS.

We recommended and the CLO and CGSO agreed to, henceforth, furnish
immediately the CAO with the Deeds of Donation, land titles,
Appraisal Committee Valuation Report and other supporting documents as
bases in recording donated properties in the books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to avail of the remedies
provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160 to enforce collection of delinquent
taxes and fill up vacancies, especially for core positions at the CTO to augment
its manpower requirement.

We recommended and the City Accountant and the City Treasurer agreed to
immediately release the 30% share from the basic RPT to the 30 barangays
pursuant to Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160. We further recommended and the
BAO personnel agreed to prepare immediately the Summary of RPTs and the
related supporting documents for the release of the 30% RPT share to the
barangays.

We recommended and Management agreed to increase the fidelity bonds of the
DOs to an amount sufficient to cover their maximum accountabilities, in
accordance with the revised schedule of bond premium rates provided by the
Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended and Management agreed to deduct from the salaries and other
benefits accruing to its employees authorized and other deductions which in no
case shall reduce their monthly net take home pay to an amount prescribed under
the existing law.

We recommended and Management agreed to designate a responsible
employee to prepare a Five-Year BP and FSSE report for each LEE in
accordance with the LEE Manual as basis for the LCE and Sangguniang
Panlungsod in coming up with a decision on whether to continue the LEE
operations or other related actions to make them financially self-sufficient and
eventually profitable to ensure the efficient and effective provision of basic
services and facilities to the constituents.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the CPDO and
City Engineer to fast track the implementation of projects under the 20% DF by
addressing the causes of delay, like insufficient funds, issues on road right-of-
way, lack of POW, and requiring the barangays to implement their projects. We
further recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, properly plan the
projects for inclusion in the AIP and budget allocation, and closely monitor project
implementation.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed that, henceforth, the
unexpended prior year’s balance of the LDRRMF for QRF and MOOE be
transferred from the General Fund to the Trust Fund at year-end pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) comply with the
documentary requirements for all disbursements in accordance with Section
4(6) of PD No. 1445; (b) prepare a well-planned procurement program to
ensure adherence to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR; (c) refrain
from incurring expenses without appropriations; and (d) exercise due
prudence in the use of the government’s scarce resources in order to avoid
unnecessary expenditures and henceforth, adhere strictly with the provisions
of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We recommended and the CEO agreed to cause the timely submission of the
COCs and reports on the disbursement for fuel and lubricants component of the
various projects in order to fully liquidate the funds received and/or return to the
SA the unspent transferred funds for completed projects.
We recommended that AAR Management implement the following courses of action to establish the accuracy/validity of the PPE accounts in the ensuing year:
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted a report on the physical count conducted on February to December 2018.
observations.reason: Physical Count of PPE conducted as of December 31, 2018 was incomplete due to lack of complete documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant: • Review all outstanding reports as the list of outstanding payable aged more than two years and those with negative balances were revert those which are not aged two years or more; negative balances.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office had reviewed and validated the Accounts Payable. Payables over payable as well as dormant accounts reverted and adjusted per JEV No. 100-2018-07-0213.
observations.reason: Verification of the financial statements and its supporting schedules showed that negative balances were still contained in dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: • Management require the officials and employees to observe prompt liquidation of cash advances by sending out another demand letters signed both by the City Accountant and by the City Mayor or his authorized representative; • The Accounting Office verify and analyze the negative balances noted in the Due from Officers and Employees. If there was over-settlement of cash advances or error in recording, effect the necessary adjustments; and • The City Accountant request write-off for long outstanding cash advances, especially of those who are deceased pursuant of the guidelines/procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19 2016.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office. A summary list to effect the mandatory payroll deductions pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 was being prepared.
observations.reason: During the mid-month of CY 2017 death certificates of some deceased officials and employees were secured to support the request.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant review and analyze the list of outstanding checks and regularly draw a JEV for the cancellation of stale checks to reflect the correct monthly balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Partial adjustment in the amount of ₱5,038.00 was effected.
observations.reason: Some checks were in the hands of the suppliers/ creditors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant (a) verify the reconciling items and draw a JEV to adjust all valid reconciling items pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; (b) conduct a thorough verification of the dormant bank accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments; and (c) provide copies of the bank reconciliation statements to the depository banks so that any doubtful and discrepancies noted between the records of the banks and of the City can be immediately resolved.
observations.managementAction: Various reconciling items were already reconciled with the Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP) in the amount of ₱392,470.60 on September 2018.
observations.reason: Retrieving of supporting documents, especially transactions that occurred in the prior years was difficult.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Engineer implement without delay the projects by taking appropriate action to address the cause of the delay and henceforth, implement the projects on schedule. We further recommended that the CPDC closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different PPAs of the City in accordance with the approved development plan as required of her under Section 476 (4) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Out of the 53 unimplemented projects in CY 2017, 45 projects were completed and six were on-going in CY 2018.
observations.reason: The following three projects were not implemented due to the following reasons: a) Rehabilitation of FAR Alley Store Spaces at Barangay Looc; b) Construction of Material Recovery Facilities in Various Barangays; c) Construction of Multi-purpose and Evacuation Center at Barangay Candau-ay; and other issues like lack of breakdown for lump sum appropriations, road-right-of-way, lot donation and site location, and tedious procurement requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, henceforth, utilize the 20% EDF in accordance with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Adhered to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management expedite the implementation of all projects, programs and activities under the LDRRMF to strengthen the capacity of the City Government to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects of disasters or calamity.
observations.managementAction: The Local Disaster Risk Management Officer (LDRRMO) created a team to regularly monitor all activities, projects and Programs funded under the LDRRMF. Reports were prepared and were submitted to the LDRRMC in some instances.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower in the LDRRMO, monitoring of projects, programs and activities funded under the LDRRMF were sometimes not given priority.
