observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRR Officer submit the monthly report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF monthly for submission on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Starting January 2018, reports on sources and utilization of LDRRM were already submitted monthly to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately stop the unauthorized use of GAD funds and, henceforth, include in their Annual GAD Plan and Budget PPAs address gender issues only.
observations.managementAction: Adhered to the provisions of RA 10121 and other existing regulations. (Note: text shows adherence to RA 10121 in prior row; for GAD, Management had already stopped utilizing GAD funds to those expenditures which were not gender-related.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management utilize BuB Funds for the implementation of the identified priority poverty reduction projects to avoid incurring unnecessary expenses which are not essential to the attainment of the objectives of the BuB Program.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped utilizing BuB funds to programs and projects which were not essential to the attainment of the objectives of the BuB Program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: • Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003. Meantime, take action to convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific timeframe; • Enforce strictly the City’s projects and programs relative to solid waste management and ensure 100% compliance thereof; and • Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: a) Management, for the mean time had controlled the dumping of garbage at the dumpsite; b) Funds for the establishment of a sanitary landfill was already allocated; c) Management hired Integrated Solid Waste Management personnel to strictly enforce the City’s projects and programs relative to solid waste management.
observations.reason: Still on the process of clearing and site preparation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management order the SDO personnel to desist from receiving payments for loan amortization from people’s organizations and to instruct all borrowers to directly pay to the CTO. We also recommended that the City Treasurer prepare a separate report of collections together with duplicate copies of the official receipt to the Accounting Office for posting in the individual subsidiary ledgers of the borrowers. We further recommended that the City Accountant immediately send demand letters/notices of demand letters to debtors to ascertain collectability of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer and the Internal Audit Section of the City Mayor’s Office conducted investigation on the alleged collection of loan amortization by some personnel of the Sectoral Desk Office (SDO). The SDO was advised to desist from collecting loan repayments from the various People’s Organizations with existing loans from the City.
observations.reason: Only four organizations had fully paid and the rest of the organizations/debtors remained dormant. There were difficulties locating members due to dissolution of associations and unlocated beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the DSWD to observe existing policy on monitoring activities to immediately address any problem, and to ensure loan amortization payment in accordance with the loan payment schedule and achieve program objective. We also recommended that the City Accountant send demand letters to SKAs to settle their loan repayments and effect rollback of the seed capital assistance.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to SKAs and the City Social Worker made follow-up of loan repayments through the conduct of FGDs and meetings.
observations.reason: Some SEA-K members still failed to pay their obligations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the conduct of complete physical count of PPEs as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21 dated February 17, 2016. Results of the physical inventory should be reconciled with the accounting and property records to present accurate balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City conducted physical inventory. Results of the physical inventory should be reconciled with the accounting and property records. The City General Services Office inventory team worked from time to time on the reconciliation of their records with the Accounting Office. The City sought authority from the City Council to write off unidentified PPE balances.
observations.reason: Physical Count conducted as of February to December 2018 was incomplete due to lack of complete documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management henceforth, instruct the City Accountant to require from the leagues the submission of the list of development projects to be funded before releasing the financial aid and assistance, and thereafter, require the submission of liquidation reports thereof. We further recommended that the City Treasurer require the Treasurer of the respective leagues to acknowledge their payments with General Form No. 51A to ensure that funds are properly accounted for subject to the usual government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office subjected the financial aid/assistance liquidation reports to review. The Treasurer of the respective leagues submitted acknowledgement of payments and the City Accounting Office performed liquidation procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant immediately submit all CY 2016 DVs, payrolls and the corresponding reports, by assigning additional personnel to segregate the DVs and payrolls with the supporting documents in three complete sets, including one for their own file, pursuant to the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, instead of relying on the scanner machines.
observations.managementAction: All CY 2016 disbursement vouchers and payrolls and the corresponding reports were already submitted to COA. 2nd copy of DVs and payrolls following years were already retained by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management a) prioritize the finding of a suitable location and allocation of funds for the establishment of the sanitary landfill, b) strictly enforce waste segregation at the source; and c) implement the recommendation contained under Finding No. 15 by assigning enforcers to stop dumpsite scavenging, and providing other means of livelihood to scavengers taking into consideration their occupational skills.
observations.managementAction: The City had purchased a lot for the establishment of the sanitary landfill. The City also conducted IEC and awareness campaign and hired ISWM enforcers.
observations.reason: Waste segregation at source was not strictly enforced in every barangay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the use of the QRF for activities not predominantly focused on the provision of immediate relief to disaster victims and henceforth, adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 10121 and other existing regulations to ensure proper utilization of the LDRRMF. We further recommended that management take action to press on the construction of Material Recovery Facilities (MRFs) in the barangays to ease out waste segregation, assign enforcers to stop dumpsite scavenging, and provide other means of livelihood to scavengers that will give them enduring advantage.
observations.managementAction: Strict adherence to the provisions of RA 10121 and on the proper use of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) was being observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: • Instruct the City Accountant and the heads of Departments to enjoin all officials and employees to observe prompt liquidation of cash advances pursuant to Executive Order No. 248, PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; • Enforce appropriate administrative and/or legal sanction provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated March 22, 2012; and • Request write-off for long outstanding cash advances, especially of those who are no longer connected with the government/and or deceased and whose whereabouts are unknown, pursuant to 2009 Revised Rules of Procedure of the Commission on Audit under COA Resolution No. 2012-001 dated March 22, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to concern officials and employees several times. The summary list to effect the mandatory payroll deductions was being prepared. Gathering and reviewing of available supporting documents and identification of officials and employees had just started for possible request for write-off.
observations.reason: Necessary documents to facilitate analysis and requests for copies of supporting documents, especially for transactions that occurred in the prior years, was difficult.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit travel orders for his travels listed in Annex A to the audit team and travels outside the province and henceforth, secure permission from the Provincial Governor every time he travels outside the Province of Negros Oriental pursuant to Section 96(b) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Travel Order from the Provincial Governor was requested to support the travels outside the province undertaken by the former Local Chief Executive.
observations.reason: Waiting for information on compliance by the concerned official.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (i) conduct regular monitoring and assessment of the targets and strategies as stated in the SWMP to ensure the efficient management of solid wastes; (ii) enforce strictly the City Ordinances and strategies to ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; and (iii) involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of the 10-Year SWMP in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Regular monitoring and assessment were conducted to ensure the efficient management of solid wastes. The enforcement involved all barangays of the City.
observations.reason: On-going formulation of strategies for the management of solid wastes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the monthly report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 COA Circular No. 2012-002. We further recommended that the CDRRM Council include in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reports on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM were submitted to the Audit Team. Programs, projects and activities to be funded out of the unexpended LDRRMF were already included in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Legal Officer in coordination with the City General Services Officer initiate the titling of the real properties to avoid possible ownership disputes in the future and to enable the accounting office to record their cost in the books of accounts; and instruct the City Accountant to ensure that all recorded transactions have been adequately supported by valid documents and to maintain subsidiary ledgers to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Legal Office initiated the titling procedures with the Register of Deeds on lots wherein deeds of donation or sale executed by the former owners have been found, as well as the payment of its taxes to the BIR. The City Accounting Office is currently seeking ways to improve their existing system. The City was waiting for the deeds of donation/lot titles/documents from the City Legal and General Services offices establishing ownership of the properties, which will be their basis to proceed with the titling procedures.
observations.reason: Necessary documents pertaining to some properties which were in the possession of implementing offices were still being prepared by the CLO for purposes of paying the required taxes with the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the City Accountant, Budget Officer, City Treasurer and all other officials responsible for the improper charges against the 5% LDRRMF to refund the amount totaling P1,125,112.85 and henceforth, adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 in the use of the 5% LDRRMF to avoid audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Noted. The Accounting Office subjected the matter to review. Awaiting Management’s action on the restitution of ₱1,125,112.85 thru transfer from the General Fund to Trust Fund under the LDRRMF continuing appropriation.
observations.reason: Implementation action pending management’s decision.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City desist from disbursing the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) balance of P1,941,164.20 and immediately return the total amount to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Management has suspended disbursements charged against the PDAF and had returned portion of the unexpended balance in the amount of ₱935,886.84 to the Bureau of Treasury. The undelivered/cancelled items before the Certification of Completion from the implementing offices would be returned to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: Awaiting the Certificate of Completion from the implementing offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fully and immediately comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern, and to always review the report and check the signboards before putting them up to ensure the correctness of all information thereon.
observations.managementAction: Management had put up tarpaulin signage in every project site. The City also ensured review of the report and check the signboards before putting them up to ensure correctness of all information.
observations.reason: Some tarpaulins lacked information like date project started, expected date of completion, etc.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the PDAF utilized for intelligence purposes, immediate refund of the amount of P500,000.00 and submission of the liquidation documents to COA Central Office for audit and action.
observations.managementAction: Refunds were made and settlement had been done in July 2012. Discussion was done by the top Management that the amount of ₱500,000.00 will be restituted from the General Fund-Unappropriated Surplus. Notice of Disallowance was on appeal.
observations.reason: Notice of Disallowance is on appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable for the abovementioned disbursements immediately restitute the same to the government and submit a written explanation/justification why they should not be penalized in accordance with the provisions of RA 3019.
observations.managementAction: Refunds were made under OR#2099027 dated November 21, 2018. The Notice of Disallowance was lifted thru NSSDC No. 18-001/ND-14-002-300-(2013) dated December 10, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately undertake the recommendations of the Technical Working Group to improve the insulation of the procured materials to make the storage facility operational/functional. Otherwise, determine the official/staff responsible/liable and impose appropriate administrative sanction.
observations.managementAction: Management appropriated an amount for the insulation improvement of the facility. Rebidding and opening of bids was scheduled on April 25, 2019. The facility remained unutilized and non-operational.
observations.reason: The facility remains unutilized and non-operational to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office (a) verify the reconciling items and draw a JEV to adjust all valid reconciling items pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; (b) conduct a thorough verification of the dormant bank accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments; and (c) provide copies of the bank reconciliation statements to the depository banks so that any doubtful and discrepancies noted between the records of the banks and of the City can be immediately resolved.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of various bank accounts had been prioritized. In December 2016, bank reconciling items amounting to ₱392,470.60 were reconciled and cleared in both the bank and books.
observations.reason: Reconciliation was difficult due to unavailability of documents and records; additional reconciling items were cleared in the amount of ₱392,470.60.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office give priority and intensify the review and analysis of the existing reconciling items and adjust those which are clearly identified, based on the existing bank statements, including the set-up of accountability for the double issuance of check replacement.
observations.managementAction: Partial adjustments had been made. The Accounting Office already made several reconciliations of accounts.
observations.reason: Reconciliation took some time because the City Accounting Office had to work back from prior years' records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant analyze and validate the dormant accounts and particularly accounts for “ID” classified under Due from NGOs/POs and the unsupported balances reflected in subsidiary ledgers. If the analysis/review of the accounts is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances from the Commission on Audit, in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: Identification of dormant accounts and investigation on the debtors was on-going. Demand letters/notices of demand letters were sent to debtors to ascertain collectability of receivables.
observations.reason: Balance as of December 31, 2018 had decreased slightly. Gathering of records to support prior year transactions was still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant immediately submit the audited Statement of Liquidation to the various source agencies for proper reporting and accounting of the funds received and utilized, and to return any unutilized amount pursuant to the terms and conditions of the MOA and COA Circular No. 94-013; and monitor regularly the status/liquidation of funds received from NGAs based on duly documented transactions and to keep a separate and complete file of documents/reports for each fund received.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office had been monitoring the fund and ensuring liquidation. The status and liquidation of projects and activities with no movement for quite a time were being investigated and verified.
observations.reason: The unexpended balance could not be returned immediately because they had to wait for the implementing agency to issue the certificates of completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: • Conduct review and reconciliation of all Due to Other NGAs accounts by ascertaining the status of project/activities completion, balances and status of liquidation; and • Require the City Accountant to likewise verify and investigate the status of the funds by confirming the balances with the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The City had been monitoring the accounts. The status and liquidation of CY 2008 accounts with no movement for quite a time were being investigated and verified.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers of the Due to NGAs accounts were not updated. There were no entries for CY 2015 because the personnel in-charge were overloaded and lacked time to carry out all responsibilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately release the RPT shares due to the barangays. Henceforth, adherence to Section 272 (d) of the Local Government Code is encouraged and require the City Accountant to determine the actual status of the 50% share of the community taxes and if found to be erroneously recorded, cause the transfer of the amount to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to be used for other activities/programs.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office had been monitoring the fund and ensuring the timely release of barangay shares. Gathering of documents to determine the correctness of the account was on-going.
observations.reason: The 50% Community Taxes amounting ₱512,614.98 remained dormant since CY 2006 to present; gathering of documents to determine the correctness of the account was ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant be required to determine the composition of the amount set up as Items In Transit converted from the in-process accounts in 2002 as well as the payments or liquidations thereof, and to adjust the Items in Transit account in order to fairly present the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation and identification of Items in Transit converted from the in-process accounts in 2002 was on-going.
observations.reason: Determination of the composition of the amount set up during conversion of accounts to PPSAS in CY 2016 was ongoing; retrieving related records from prior years was challenging.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant be required to request from the GSO for a list of depreciable assets acquired from 2002 to 2008 to compute the depreciation expense for all depreciable assets for the current audit period as well as from 2002 up to 2006 using the Prior Years’ Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation accounts in order to fairly present the asset and equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office had requested the necessary schedules and was compiling the data. Identification of depreciable assets purchased from 2002 to 2007 was ongoing.
observations.reason: Identification of depreciable assets purchased from CY 2002 to 2007 was ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider revising the rental rates of stall spaces in the public market to make it a viable enterprise and in the process, take into consideration the volume of trade or business in each section in order to come up with more equitable and just rates, and avoid charging to market operations salaries and other emoluments of personnel who are not actually rendering services therein.
observations.managementAction: An ordinance was passed by the City Council on July 4, 2002 imposing a 25% adjustment on all new stall awardees and five percent increase every three years thereafter on the rental rates of the stall spaces at the Dumaguete City Public Market Complex. Implementation of the ordinance was effected in January 2011 among new stall awardees.
observations.reason: The ordinance was implemented only to new stall awardees. Increase of rental rates had not been imposed to the old stallholders since 1988.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant be required to identify the composition of the portion of government equity representing the costs of the different completed projects and fixed assets and transfer the same to the General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The assigned personnel at the City Accounting Office had been working on the liquidation of completed projects under Trust Fund with the offices implementing the projects.
observations.reason: Verification with implementing offices and preparation of liquidation reports on completed projects was on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Agriculturist be required to report monthly all births of animals owned by the City, to brand all animals purchased or produced, to require livestock technicians to be on call at all times to attend to sick animals, and to submit utilization reports for feeds and vitamins. Moreover, require the City Accountant to maintain livestock ledger cards and to update these monthly using the reports submitted by the City Agriculturist, and require, as well, the GSO to maintain property cards, to require Certificates of Ownership or Transfer for large cattle to be purchased, which should be filed as reference for identifying each animal.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary ledgers were maintained by the Accounting Office and updated monthly. The City General Services Office inventory team maintained records of animals under dispersal programs; GSO and Agriculturist Office were coordinating.
observations.reason: Some necessary documents were not available to facilitate complete reconciliation; some records were incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the City Accountant to effect the appropriate adjustments to reconcile accounts Due to Other Funds and Due From Other Funds.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of accounts Due to Other Funds (2-03-01-010) and Due from Other Funds (1-03-04-050) was on-going. Reconciliation took time but was being prioritized.
observations.reason: Reconciliation took time because the City Accounting Office had to work back from the prior years' records and some supporting documents were incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that management implement the following courses of action
to establish the accuracy/validity of the PPE accounts in the ensuing year:
a) Direct the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the physical
inventory of properties and prepare the RPCPPE;
b) Direct the City Accountant and City General Services Officer to reconcile
their records with the RPCPPE not later than September 30, 2018 and effect
the necessary adjustments in either the property records or in the books of
accounts; and
c) Require the Accounting Office to identify items marked as “for
identification” and to update the PPELCs as this subsidiary record shall
support the PPE controlling account balance.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the City Accountant:
a) Review all outstanding payables, validate available supporting documents, and
revert those which are not supported with valid claims and those which are
aged two years or more;
b) Investigate accounts payable which have remained dormant and those with
negative balances and determine the cause/s and prepare adjusting journal
entries for all errors noted; and
c) Henceforth, record accounts payable in accordance with the regulations
provided in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and maintain
subsidiary ledgers indicating the name of creditor including the address to
allow confirmation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that:
a) Management require the immediate liquidation of cash advances by sending
out another demand letters signed both by the City Accountant and by the City
Mayor or his authorized representative;
b) The Accounting Office verify and analyze the negative balances noted in the
Due from Officers and Employees. If there was over-settlement of cash
advances or error in recording, effect the necessary adjustments; and
c) The City Accountant request write-off for long outstanding cash advances,
esspecially of those who are deceased pursuant of the guidelines/procedures set
forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant review and analyze the list of
outstanding checks and regularly draw a JEV for the cancellation of stale checks to
reflect the correct monthly balances of the affected accounts in the financial
statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant (a) verify the reconciling items and
draw a JEV to adjust all valid reconciling items pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; (b) conduct a thorough verification of the
dormant bank accounts and to effect the necessary adjustments; and (c) provide copies
of the bank reconciliation statements to the depository banks so that any discrepancies
noted between the records of the banks and of the City can be immediately resolved.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit travel orders for his travels listed in Annex A to the audit team and henceforth, secure permission from the Provincial Governor every time he travels outside of the Province of Negros Oriental pursuant to Section 96(b) of the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The former Local Chief Executive did not submit travel orders of his travels.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, payrolls and other relevant reports pursuant to Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-06 and Section 6.5 of COA circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Office lacked personnel to do the sorting and scanning of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the finding of a suitable location and allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003. In the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dumpsite into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The City is pursuing on the possibility of establishing a Waste-to-Energy facility through a Private-Public Partnership (PPP). A study and discussions with the private service provider on this matter are still ongoing. Once the facility is in place, the old dumpsite will be closed and rehabilitated. Also, the Sangguniang Panlungsod has yet to pass a PPP ordinance, before a MOA is drawn.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the monthly report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to monitor disbursements. Furthermore, we recommended that the CDRRM Council include in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The LDRRMO did not submit monthly Disaster Risk Reduction report on the Sources and Utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) Management stop the practice of holding receptions or serving meals and snacks during cultural, sports and other events and the charging thereof to government funds other than to the representation allowance of officials concerned; b) management refrain from incurring expenditures which are for religious and private purposes; c) require officials concerned to refund the expenses incurred during the Santacruzan and other activities for religious and private purposes; and d) henceforth, strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-003 and Administrative Order No. 103 is highly recommended.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Transactions during the year under audit showed that management still served meals and snacks during events which are not for public purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the City Accountant to effect the appropriate adjustment to reconcile accounts Due to Other Funds and Due From Other Funds.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Documents are incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management order the SDO personnel to desist from receiving
payments for loan amortization from people’s organizations and to instruct all
borrowers to directly pay to the CTO. We also recommended that the City Treasurer
prepare a separate report of collections together with duplicate copies of the official
receipt to the Accounting Office for posting in the individual subsidiary ledgers of the
borrowers. We further recommended that the City Accountant immediately send
demand letters to debtors to ascertain collectability of receivables.

We recommended that management require the DSWD to observe existing policy on
program monitoring activities to immediately address any problem, ensure loan
amortization payment in accordance with the loan payment schedule and achieve
program objective. We also recommended that the City Accountant send demand
letters to SKAs to settle their monthly/quarterly/semi-annually rollback of the seed
capital assistance.

We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007, in the granting of financial
assistance to NGOs/POs. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance
of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of
transactions.

We recommended that management collaborate with other concerned agencies, like
the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE), Department of Interior and Local
Government (DILG) and Technical Education and Skills Development (TESDA),
and conduct regular reporting, monitoring and evaluation of the program to address
local unemployment.

We recommended that management exercise prudence in the use of
TraBAJO funds. Henceforth, implement the program in accordance with DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2016-056 dated April 26, 2016.

We recommended that management, henceforth, comply with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We recommended that management, henceforth, conduct their trainings, seminars,
workshops or similar activities within the City to avoid wastage of government funds
and instead, use scarce resources to finance priority development projects.

We recommended that management exercise due prudence in the use of the
government’s scarce resources in order to avoid unnecessary and extravagant
expenditures. Henceforth, adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No.
2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and the austerity measures enumerated under
Administrative Order No. 103.

We recommended that management prioritize the conduct of complete physical
count of PPEs as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2016-21 dated
February 17, 2016 and other existing regulations. Results of the physical inventory
should be reconciled with the accounting and property records to present accurate
balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management require the City Accountant to submit to the COA
Audit Team Leader the DVs covering the payment of cash advances for
confidential/intelligence purposes seven days after the release of check as
prescribed in Section 6.1.8.4 of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular
No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that management, henceforth, instruct the City Accountant to
require from the leagues the submission of the list of development projects to be
funded before releasing the financial aid and assistance, and thereafter, require the
submission of liquidation reports thereof. We also recommended that the City
Treasurer require the treasurer of the respective leagues to acknowledge their
payments with General Form No. 51A to ensure that funds are properly accounted
for subject to the usual government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the City Accountant immediately submit all CY 2016 DVs,
payrolls and the corresponding reports, by assigning additional personnel to
segregate the DVs and payrolls with the supporting documents in three complete
sets, including one for their own file, pursuant to the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, instead of relying on the
scanner machines.

We recommended that management: a) Instruct the City Accountant and the heads of Departments to enjoin all officials and employees to observe prompt liquidation of cash advances pursuant to Executive Order No. 248, P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; b) Enforce appropriate administrative and/or legal sanction provided under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated March 22, 2012; and c) Request write-off for long outstanding cash advances, especially of those who are no longer connected with the government/and or deceased and whose whereabouts are unknown, pursuant to 2009 Revised Rules of Procedure of the Commission on Audit under COA Resolution No. 2012-001 dated March 22, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management sent demand letters to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances. The Accounting Office prepared a summary list of officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances which had decreased from P27,825,745.45 in December 2015 to P26,479,894.92 in December 2016 or a decrease of P1,345,850.53 or 4.83%.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers are no longer in the service due to retirement, transfer, resignation and death. Others simply did not mind to settle their cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: (i) allow only duly designated disbursing officers or persons given explicit authority by the Head of Office to draw cash advances for operating expenses or for special purposes; (ii) monitor and follow-up application for bond of officials and employees of the City entrusted with public funds to ensure that government funds are safeguarded from loss or misappropriation; and (iii) refrain from granting the Local Chief Executive cash advances other than for travel expenses pursuant to Section 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Management already refrained from granting cash advances to non-bonded officials/employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: refrain from granting the Local Chief Executive cash advances other than for travel expenses; observe the provisions embodied in Executive Order No. 248, as amended by EO No. 248-A, and EO No. 298 when undertaking foreign travels.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: A justification/report on the immediate and direct benefits derived by the City from the travel was submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exercise prudence in the disbursement of funds and henceforth, stop the practice of utilizing government funds for beauty pageants and other forms of entertainment during fiesta celebrations, charter day anniversary and other similar events, to avoid wastage of the scarce resources of the city government.
observations.managementAction: Effective CY 2016, management did not use government funds for the Miss Dumaguete Pageant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that : a) The City Accountant coordinate with the Human Resource Officer and the GSIS to facilitate reconciliation of the unremitted premiums and loan repayments due to GSIS, and immediately report changes; and b) Henceforth, the City Treasurer deduct from the salaries of member employees all mandatory premium contributions and loan payments due to GSIS, and immediately report to GSIS changes in the member’s information, if any, every month as they take place as provided under the pertinent sections of R.A. 8291.
observations.managementAction:
The City Human Resource Management Office and the City Accounting Office worked together to deduct from the salaries of employees the amount for their GSIS contributions and loan repayments. Per certification issued by the GSIS, collection efficiency rate of the City has increased in CY 2016, as follows:
Particulars 2015 2016
Premiums 101.59% 102.77%
Loans 75.16% 97.03%

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management utilize the 20% development fund only for viable and sustainable development programs and projects enumerated under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Development projects were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, particularly the City Engineer’s Office, hasten the implementation of projects/ programs/ activities to attain socio-economic development as intended in the establishment of the 20% development fund.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer’s Office implemented the projects. However, out of the 102-programmed projects, two were not implemented due to problem with the site.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB refrain from charging expenses not listed under Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and its related DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars and review the programs/activities/projects included in the SEF budget to ensure proper utilization thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB terminate the time deposits and use the Fund for the priority needs of public schools, such as repairs of school buildings, facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals and sports development in accordance with Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer already terminated the Time Deposit under the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: (i) conduct regular monitoring and assessment of the targets and strategies as stated in the 10-Year SWMP to ensure the efficient management of the solid wastes; (ii) enforce strictly the City Ordinances and strategies to ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; and (iii) involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of the 10-Year SWMP in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Close coordination with the City engineer’s Office was observed to fast track the liquidation of the transferred funds. Segregation at source and segregated collection were done where biodegradable wastes were delivered to a farm and were being processed for the farmers’ needs. Also, a new Waste Characterization Study was conducted by the ENRO. The reimplementation of SWMP started at the Dumaguete City Market Complex on April 1, 2017. Segregation at source and segregated collection were done.
observations.reason: The MRFs in some barangays still have to be equipped and rehabilitated to make them functional.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (i) refrain from distributing its relief items intended for calamity purposes to CSWDO clients covered under different programs; (ii) refrain from using its fuel stock on activities not related to calamity preparedness, prevention and/or mitigation; and (iii) henceforth, adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and its related National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Joint Memorandum Circulars in the utilization of the 5% LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the purchase of non-basic emergency items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management in formulating GAD Plans and Budget, gender issues that ought to be addressed be first determined and include therein PPAs that would address such issues and concerns in accordance with JMC No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: Management has started the formulation of a new GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: (i) require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of all properties of the City at least once a year; (ii) require the General Services Office (GSO) to prepare and submit to the auditor a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than January 31 of each year and reconcile it with the accounting records; and (iii) the Accounting Office and GSO to plan and/or devise a system that will facilitate the quick updating and reconciliation of records of the City in the accounting of Government’s properties.
observations.managementAction: The GSO conducted the physical count of properties and submitted the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the audit team.
observations.reason: The results on the physical count of property, plant and equipment were not reconciled with records of the Accounting Office. The GSO reported a total of P313,412,618.55 while the PPE account had P1,254,392,429.31, a difference of P940,979,810.76. The GSO was not informed of the conversion of accounts under the PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Require the City Legal Officer in coordination with the City General Services Officer to initiate the titling of the real properties to avoid possible ownership disputes in the future and to enable the accounting office to record their cost in the books of accounts giving priority to cases that have Electronic Certificate Authorizing Registration (ECAR). b) Instruct the City Accountant to ensure that all recorded transactions have been adequately supported by valid documents and to maintain subsidiary ledgers to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and c) Likewise, require the General Services Office to maintain complete records and documents of all the properties of the City Government pursuant to Section 490 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The CLO is currently carrying out the review of records on the Deeds of Sale, Deeds of Donation/Conveyances. Titling of the lands donated or sold to the City is being expedited giving priority to cases that have Electronic Certificate Authorizing Registration (ECAR). Lots with newly issued titles in the name of the City were forwarded to the GSO for safekeeping. The GSO is keeping a record of all real properties based on the Transfer Certificates of Titles from the CLO.
observations.reason: Available subsidiary ledgers of real properties are only from CY 2008 and onwards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant, Budget Officer, City Treasurer and all other officials responsible for the improper charges against the 5% LDRRMF refund the amount totaling P1,125,112.85 and henceforth, adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 in the use of the 5% LDRRMF to avoid audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management limit the use of the Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund to programs/projects/activities that are aligned with the guidelines contained under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 otherwise, the same may be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the purchase of non-basic emergency items.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City desist from disbursing the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) and immediately return the total amount to the Bureau of Treasury. A portion of the unexpended balance amounting to P678,073.76 was already returned to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Management had put up funds and a portion of the unexpended balance amounting to P678,073.76 was returned to the Bureau of Treasury. Moreover, the reconciliation and liquidation of 15 PDAF projects is still ongoing. The deadline is set at the end of CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Human Resource Management Officer revisit the City’s policy on the hiring of casual employees and those under job order and contract of service; carefully assess the necessity of their hiring; limit the number to what is needed, seeing to it that their duties do not duplicate those of regular employees; and henceforth, the Local Chief Executive and the rest of the City officials observe strictly the existing laws, rules and regulations on the hiring of emergency employees, otherwise, expenses related thereto will be disallowed in audit for being unnecessary, irregular and illegal.
observations.managementAction: The newly appointed City Government Assistant Department Head I is undertaking some measures to address the issues raised in the audit observation.
observations.reason: The Office of Human Resource is implementing measures; full resolution pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management instruct all drivers to refrain from using government motor vehicles without the Driver’s Trip Ticket duly approved by the Administrative Official authorizing the official travel, to summarize in chronological order all trip tickets for each vehicle issued during the month in a Monthly Report of Official Travel and the OIC-General Service Officer to consolidate the Monthly Report of Official Travels who shall prepare and submit to the auditor a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.
observations.managementAction: The GSO made sure that the use of vehicles was covered with trip tickets. The Office prepared monthly reports for some departments.
observations.reason: The Monthly Report of Fuel consumption submitted to the audit team during the year under audit lacks necessary data that should have been reflected in the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant require, among others sufficient copies of supporting papers before certifying the DV, assign specific personnel to segregate the DVs and payrolls including the supporting documents in three complete sets, and employ such other measures to enable them to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers and payrolls.
observations.managementAction: The City Office already purchased three scanners. The City Accounting Office purchased Box A of three scanners.
observations.reason: The office lacked personnel to operate the third scanner.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fully and immediately comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to promote transparency and accountability, secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern, and avoid the penalty clause therein; always review the report and check the signboards before putting them up to ensure correctness of all information thereon.
observations.managementAction: The City has put up the tarpaulin signage in the project sites.
observations.reason: The tarpaulin did not contain vital information, like the start and completion dates of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require concerned officials/employees to refund the full amount so disbursed/collected out of the financial subsidy; utilize the remaining balance of the financial subsidy amounting to P357,381.33 in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-03; and submit the Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and DILG.
observations.managementAction: Already replenished and transferred to Trust Fund per OR No. 610869.

observations.recommendation: For the PDAF utilized for intelligence purposes, we recommended for the immediate refund of the amount of P500,000.00 so that these could be used for bona fide pro-poor projects and programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: The practice was already suspended. The Notice of Disallowance (ND) is on appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the persons liable for the above-mentioned disbursements: a) Immediately restitute the same to the government; and b) Submit a written explanation/justification why they should not be penalized in accordance with the provisions of R.A. 3019.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a written explanation why they should not be penalized; an appeal was filed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately undertake the recommendations of the Technical Working Group to make the necessary adjustments of the room volume to provide adequate insulation. Likewise, undertake all necessary measures, if still possible to make the storage facility operational/functional. Otherwise, determine the official/staff responsible/liable and impose appropriate administrative sanction.
observations.managementAction: Management appropriated P660,000.00 in CY 2017 for the improvement of the Cold Storage Facility.
observations.reason: The facility remains unutilized and non-operational during the year under audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: immediately require the City Accountant to give priority to and intensify the review and analysis of the existing reconciling items and adjust those which have not been confirmed by the depository banks; and intensify the identification of double issuances and issuance of replacements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already made several requests from the banks for the copies of bank statements. Reconciliations amounting to P1,166,381.42 were already reconciled and cleared in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Verification with the banks for copies of bank statements from CY 1991 is still ongoing due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation and substantiation of the bank accounts which have not been confirmed by the depository banks and to set up accountability for the double issuance of check replacement.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already made several requests from the banks for the copies of bank statements for reconciliation. Reconciliations amounting to P1,166,381.42 were already reconciled and cleared in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Verification with the banks for copies of bank statements from CY 1991 is still ongoing due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant analyze and validate the dormant accounts and accounts for “ID” classified under Due from NGOs/POs and the unsupported balances reflected in the subsidiary ledgers. If the analysis/review of the accounts is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances from the Commission on Audit, in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: Management verified the status and liquidation of account balances. The Accounting Office has been monitoring the identification of dormant accounts and is in the process of reconciling the balances.
observations.reason: Identification of dormant accounts was given limited time and personnel; available records are incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Immediately submit the audited Statement of Liquidation to the various source agencies for proper reporting and accounting of the funds received and utilized and to return any unutilized amount pursuant to the terms and conditions of the MOA and COA Circular No. 94-013; and b) Monitor regularly the status/liquidation of all funds by confirming the balances with the source agencies and keep a separate and complete file of documents/reports for each fund received.
observations.managementAction: Close coordination with the City Engineer’s Office was observed to fast track the liquidation of the transferred funds. The implementing offices are being coordinated. A portion of the unexpended balance could not be returned immediately because they have to wait for the implementing agency to issue the certificate of completion on the completed projects. They also have to obtain clearance from the City Mayor to return the balance before the liquidation and refund of any unexpended balance could be done.
observations.reason: The unexpended balance could not be returned immediately as implementing agencies must issue certificates of completion and obtain clearance from the City Mayor; hence, liquidation and refund are pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Conduct review and reconciliation of all Due to Other NGAs accounts by ascertaining the status of project/activities completion, status of liquidation; and b) Require the City Accountant to likewise verify and investigate the status of the funds by confirming the balances with the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Management verified the status and liquidation of accounts by confirming balances with source agencies and through review of supporting documents. The Accounting Office has been monitoring the status and ensuring timely liquidation.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers (SL) of Due to NGAs account were not updated. The personnel-in-charge had several functions and lacked time to carry out all responsibilities; some documentation remained incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management observe the limitations in the appropriation of funds for Intelligence and Confidential purposes as provided under DILG Memorandum Circular Nos. 99-65 and 99-100.
observations.managementAction: The incumbent Local Chief Executive did not utilize his Confidential and Intelligence Fund during the year.

observations.recommendation: To ensure the efficient and effective implementation of similar projects and with the foregoing gaps noted, we recommended that Management: a) Emphasize the importance of maintaining sufficient, reliable, accurate and timely information by duly supporting and maintaining adequate records, e.g., MOA, minutes of meetings, validation forms, criteria/other bases, as evidence of the activities undertaken to uphold transparency and accountability and enhance the planning and decision-making process; b) Strengthen its planning, coordinating and reporting functions by maintaining close supervision on adherence to prescribed rules and regulations; c) Enhance the existing policy on project monitoring activities; d) Establish feedback, evaluation and validation mechanisms as tools to ensure efficiency in the attainment of objectives through timely and appropriate interventions to rectify deficiencies/deviations noted; and e) Henceforth, in future programs of like nature, give emphasis on the proper selection/qualification and prioritization of beneficiaries by implementing recommendations a to d above.
observations.managementAction: The recommendations were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exercise political will and give particular attention to the control and restraint of existing informal settlers, especially in disaster-prone areas, to avoid wasting government funds on recurring and deliberate damages because of their illegal occupancy on private and public lands.
observations.managementAction: The incumbent CSWDO committed to follow the audit recommendations. She explained that the Assistance Project was implemented before she was appointed as the CSWDO. She also explained that records pertaining to the project were inadequate.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation to justify the purchase of one unit refrigerator and queen size bed with accessories which were charged to SEF.
observations.managementAction: The practice was already stopped. A justification on the purchase of one unit refrigerator and a queen size bed was already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require each department to concentrate on their mandated duties and not to encroach on the functions of other departments. Thus, (i) the City Engineer shall take charge in the immediate implementation of the projects, (ii) the City Planning and Development Coordinator shall monitor and evaluate the implementation of the projects done by the City Engineer for validation purposes; and (iii) the General Services Officer shall update property records before helping out in the implementation of the projects which is the primary duty of the City Engineer.
observations.managementAction: Management already implemented the recommendations. There was coordination among offices, like the GSO, CPDO and CEO. (Note: Out of the 46 unaccounted projects; one (water line in Purok Lunoy, Brgy. Junob) was not implemented because the project was funded and implemented by a non-governmental organization.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately release the RPT shares due to the barangays. Henceforth, adherence to timely and immediate transfer of the Share of Real Property Taxes to the barangays is encouraged and require the city accountant to determine the actual status of the 50% share of the community taxes and if found to be erroneously recorded, cause the transfer of the amount to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to be used for other activities/programs.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has been monitoring the fund and ensuring the timely and immediate transfer of the Share of Real Property Taxes to the barangays.
observations.reason: The 50% Community Taxes amounting to P512,614.98 has been dormant since 2006. Documents needed to determine the correctness of the amount are being gathered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat require all department heads to prepare and submit the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as prescribed to be able to submit the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the city as required in the pertinent provisions of Section 7, Rule II of RA 9184 and require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate all data.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat and GSO are regularly consolidating the PPMPs of the different offices. Requests without the corresponding PPMPs were not processed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Agriculturist ensure strict implementation of the terms of the memorandum of agreement on swine and poultry dispersal program and initiate action against beneficiaries who have been found to have violated the terms, not only to protect the interest of the government, but to avert the failure of the program.
observations.managementAction: The incumbent City Agriculturist informed the audit team that the recommendation was already explained and complied during the time of the previous auditors. The poultry dispersal program was dissolved years ago; therefore, enforcement of the MOA against the 38 beneficiaries is now moot and academic. Moreover, whereabouts of some of the beneficiaries are now unknown.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to determine the composition of the amount set up as Items-In-Transit converted from the in-process accounts in 2002 as well as the payments or liquidations thereof, and to adjust the Items In Transit account in order to fairly present the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The determination of the composition of the amount set up in 2002 during the conversion of accounts to PPSAS is still ongoing due to time constraint and lack of personnel.
observations.reason: The review and identification of composition of the Items-In-Transit balance dating back to 2002 is still ongoing; limited personnel and time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to request from the GSO a list of assets acquired from 2002 to 2007, to compute the depreciation expense for all depreciable assets for the current audit period as well as from 2002 up to 2006 using the Prior Years’ Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation accounts in order to fairly present the asset and equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation expenses for purchases of depreciable assets from 2008 onwards were recognized in 2008. The Accounting and the GSO are in the process of identifying depreciable assets purchased from CY 2002 to CY 2007.
observations.reason: Identification of depreciable assets purchased in CY 2002 to CY 2007 is still ongoing; documents are being gathered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of holding receptions or serving meals and snacks during cultural, sports and other events and the charging thereof to government funds other than to the representation allowance of officials concerned.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider revising the rental rates of stall spaces in the public market to make it a viable enterprise and in the process, take into consideration the volume of trade or business in each section in order to come up with more equitable and just rates, and avoid charging to market operations the salaries and other emoluments of personnel who are not actually rendering services therein.
observations.managementAction: The City Council passed an ordinance on July 4, 2002 imposing a 25 percent adjustment on all new stall awardees and five percent increase every three years thereafter on the rental rates of the stall spaces at the Dumaguete City Public Market Complex. The GSO and market management have been implementing some recommendations.
observations.reason: Increase of rental rates have not been applied to old stallholders since 1988; full implementation lacks support from local legislators.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant be required to identify the composition of the portion of government equity representing the costs of the different completed projects and fixed assets and transfer the same to the General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office made adjustments and the identified costs were being verified with the implementing offices and liquidation reports on completed projects are still being prepared.
observations.reason: Reconciliations and verification with implementing offices are still ongoing; liquidation reports on completed projects are still being prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Agriculturist be required to report monthly all births of animals owned by the city, to brand all purchased or produced animals, to require livestock technicians to be on call at all times to attend to sick animals, and to submit utilization reports for feeds and vitamins. Moreover, require the accountant to maintain livestock ledger cards and to update these monthly using the reports submitted by the City Agriculturist. Require the GSO to maintain property cards, to require Certificates of Ownership or Transfer for large cattle to be purchased, which should be filed as reference for identifying each animal.
observations.managementAction: The GSO Inventory Team maintains records of animals under dispersal programs based on the reports submitted by the City Agriculture’s Office. The Accounting Office is maintaining the subsidiary ledgers and updating them monthly. The City Agriculture’s Office caused the branding (ear-tagging) on purchased animals. Livestock technicians are now on call to attend the sick animals.
observations.reason: Certificates of Ownership of large cattle and the record of births of animals owned by the City were not yet fully received; utilization reports on feeds and vitamins were not regularly submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the OIC City Accountant be required to take up the correcting entry to record the disbursement of P15,800.00 as a donation to Barangay Balugo and to correct its asset account:
Account Code Debit Credit
Subsidy to LGUs 874 15,800.00
Land 201 15,800.00
It is also recommended that the barangay be required to draw a journal voucher (JV) to record the value of the land purchased, and to submit a copy of the JV to the COA for attachment to the voucher. The city accountant, treasurer, and legal officer should also be admonished to take note of this oversight in order to preclude a repetition of the same.
observations.managementAction: The adjustment was already effected per JEV No. 100-2016-06-137 dated June 30, 2016.
